title: Constitution as the fundamental law
text:
Part 1 Article 1
Clause 1
Constitution as the fundamental law
This Constitution is the fundamental law of Nepal. Any law inconsistent with this Constitution shall, to the extent of such inconsistency, be void.
raw_text: This Constitution is the fundamental law of Nepal. Any law inconsistent with this Constitution shall, to the extent of such inconsistency, be void.

title: Constitution as the fundamental law
text:
Part 1 Article 1
Clause 2
Constitution as the fundamental law
It shall be the duty of every person to observe this Constitution.
raw_text: It shall be the duty of every person to observe this Constitution.

title: Sovereignty and state authority
text:
Part 1 Article 2
Sovereignty and state authority
The sovereignty and state authority of Nepal shall be vested in the Nepalese people. It shall be exercised in accordance with the provisions set forth in this Constitution.
raw_text: The sovereignty and state authority of Nepal shall be vested in the Nepalese people. It shall be exercised in accordance with the provisions set forth in this Constitution.

title: Nation
text:
Part 1 Article 3
Nation
All the Nepalese people, with multiethnic, multilingual, multi-religious, multicultural characteristics and in geographical diversities, and having common aspirations and being united by a bond of allegiance to national independence, territorial integrity, national interest and prosperity of Nepal, collectively constitute the nation.
raw_text: All the Nepalese people, with multiethnic, multilingual, multi-religious, multicultural characteristics and in geographical diversities, and having common aspirations and being united by a bond of allegiance to national independence, territorial integrity, national interest and prosperity of Nepal, collectively constitute the nation.

title: State of Nepal
text:
Part 1 Article 4
Clause 1
State of Nepal
Nepal is an independent, indivisible, sovereign, secular, inclusive, democratic, socialism-oriented, federal democratic republican state.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "secular" means religious, cultural freedoms, including protection of religion, culture handed down from the time immemorial.
raw_text:
Nepal is an independent, indivisible, sovereign, secular, inclusive, democratic, socialism-oriented, federal democratic republican state.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "secular" means religious, cultural freedoms, including protection of religion, culture handed down from the time immemorial.

title: State of Nepal
text:
Part 1 Article 4
Clause 2
State of Nepal
The territory of Nepal shall comprise:
(a) the territory existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and
(b) such other territory as may be acquired after the commencement of this Constitution.
raw_text:
The territory of Nepal shall comprise:
(a) the territory existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and
(b) such other territory as may be acquired after the commencement of this Constitution.

title: National interest
text:
Part 1 Article 5
Clause 1
National interest
Safeguarding of the freedom, sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality, independence and dignity of Nepal, the rights of the
Nepalese people, border security, economic wellbeing and prosperity shall be the basic elements of the national interest of Nepal.
raw_text:
Safeguarding of the freedom, sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality, independence and dignity of Nepal, the rights of the
Nepalese people, border security, economic wellbeing and prosperity shall be the basic elements of the national interest of Nepal.

title: National interest
text:
Part 1 Article 5
Clause 2
National interest
Any conduct and act contrary to the national interest shall be punishable by the Federal law.
raw_text: Any conduct and act contrary to the national interest shall be punishable by the Federal law.

title: Languages of the nation
text:
Part 1 Article 6
Languages of the nation
All languages spoken as the mother tongues in Nepal are the languages of the nation.
raw_text: All languages spoken as the mother tongues in Nepal are the languages of the nation.

title: Official language
text:
Part 1 Article 7
Clause 1
Official language
The Nepali language in the Devnagari script shall be the official language of Nepal.
raw_text: The Nepali language in the Devnagari script shall be the official language of Nepal.

title: Official language
text:
Part 1 Article 7
Clause 2
Official language
A State may, by a State law, determine one or more than one languages of the nation spoken by a majority of people within the State as its official language(s), in addition to the Nepali language.
raw_text: A State may, by a State law, determine one or more than one languages of the nation spoken by a majority of people within the State as its official language(s), in addition to the Nepali language.

title: Official language
text:
Part 1 Article 7
Clause 3
Official language
Other matters relating to language shall be as decided by the Government of Nepal, on recommendation of the Language Commission.
raw_text: Other matters relating to language shall be as decided by the Government of Nepal, on recommendation of the Language Commission.

title: National flag
text:
Part 1 Article 8
Clause 1
National flag
The national flag of Nepal consists of two juxtaposed triangular figures with a crimson-coloured base and deep blue borders, there being a white emblem of the crescent moon with eight rays visible out of sixteen in the upper part and a white emblem of a twelve rayed sun in the lower part.
raw_text: The national flag of Nepal consists of two juxtaposed triangular figures with a crimson-coloured base and deep blue borders, there being a white emblem of the crescent moon with eight rays visible out of sixteen in the upper part and a white emblem of a twelve rayed sun in the lower part.

title: National flag
text:
Part 1 Article 8
Clause 2
National flag
The method of drawing out the flag and other particulars relating thereto shall be as set forth in Schedule-1.
raw_text: The method of drawing out the flag and other particulars relating thereto shall be as set forth in Schedule-1.

title: National anthem etc
text:
Part 1 Article 9
Clause 1
National anthem etc
The national anthem of Nepal shall be as set forth in Schedule-2.
raw_text: The national anthem of Nepal shall be as set forth in Schedule-2.

title: National anthem etc
text:
Part 1 Article 9
Clause 2
National anthem etc
The coat-of-arms of Nepal shall be as set forth in Schedule-3.
raw_text: The coat-of-arms of Nepal shall be as set forth in Schedule-3.

title: National anthem etc
text:
Part 1 Article 9
Clause 3
National anthem etc
The Rhododendron Arboreum shall be the national flower, Crimson Colour shall be the national colour, the Cow shall be the national animal and the Lophophorus shall be the national bird of Nepal
raw_text: The Rhododendron Arboreum shall be the national flower, Crimson Colour shall be the national colour, the Cow shall be the national animal and the Lophophorus shall be the national bird of Nepal

title: Not to deprive of citizenship
text:
Part 2 Article 10
Clause 1
Not to deprive of citizenship
No citizen of Nepal may be deprived of the right to obtain citizenship.
raw_text: No citizen of Nepal may be deprived of the right to obtain citizenship.

title: Not to deprive of citizenship
text:
Part 2 Article 10
Clause 2
Not to deprive of citizenship
There is a provision of single federal citizenship with State identity in Nepal.
raw_text: There is a provision of single federal citizenship with State identity in Nepal.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 1
To be citizens of Nepal
The persons who have obtained the citizenship of Nepal at the time of commencement of this Constitution and who are qualified to obtain citizenship in accordance with this Part shall be the citizens of Nepal.
raw_text: The persons who have obtained the citizenship of Nepal at the time of commencement of this Constitution and who are qualified to obtain citizenship in accordance with this Part shall be the citizens of Nepal.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 2
To be citizens of Nepal
The following person who has his or her permanent domicile in Nepal at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be the citizen of Nepal by descent:
(a) a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent prior to the commencement of this Constitution,
(b) a person whose father or mother was a citizen of Nepal at his or her birth.
raw_text:
The following person who has his or her permanent domicile in Nepal at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be the citizen of Nepal by descent:
(a) a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent prior to the commencement of this Constitution,
(b) a person whose father or mother was a citizen of Nepal at his or her birth.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 3
To be citizens of Nepal
A child of a citizen having obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth prior to the commencement of Nepal shall, upon attaining majority, acquire the citizenship of Nepal by descent if the child's father and mother both are citizens of Nepal.
raw_text: A child of a citizen having obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth prior to the commencement of Nepal shall, upon attaining majority, acquire the citizenship of Nepal by descent if the child's father and mother both are citizens of Nepal.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 4
To be citizens of Nepal
Every minor who is found within Nepal and the whereabouts of whose father and mother are not known shall, until the father or the mother of the child is traced, be a citizen of Nepal by descent.
raw_text: Every minor who is found within Nepal and the whereabouts of whose father and mother are not known shall, until the father or the mother of the child is traced, be a citizen of Nepal by descent.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 5
To be citizens of Nepal
A person who is born in Nepal from a woman who is a citizen of Nepal and has resided in Nepal and whose father is not traced shall be provided with the citizenship of Nepal by descent.
Provided that his or her father is held to be a foreign citizen, the citizenship of such person shall be converted into naturalized citizenship as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text:
A person who is born in Nepal from a woman who is a citizen of Nepal and has resided in Nepal and whose father is not traced shall be provided with the citizenship of Nepal by descent.
Provided that his or her father is held to be a foreign citizen, the citizenship of such person shall be converted into naturalized citizenship as provided for in the Federal law.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 6
To be citizens of Nepal
A foreign woman who has a matrimonial relationship with a citizen of Nepal may, if she so wishes, acquire the naturalized citizenship of Nepal as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: A foreign woman who has a matrimonial relationship with a citizen of Nepal may, if she so wishes, acquire the naturalized citizenship of Nepal as provided for in the Federal law.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 7
To be citizens of Nepal
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Article, in the case of a person born from a woman who is a citizen of Nepal and married to a foreign citizen, the person may acquire the naturalized citizenship of Nepal in accordance with the Federal law if he or she has permanently resided in Nepal and has not acquired the citizenship of a foreign country.
Provided that if such person's mother and father both are citizens of Nepal at the time of acquisition of citizenship, such person born in Nepal may acquire the citizenship of Nepal by descent.
raw_text:
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Article, in the case of a person born from a woman who is a citizen of Nepal and married to a foreign citizen, the person may acquire the naturalized citizenship of Nepal in accordance with the Federal law if he or she has permanently resided in Nepal and has not acquired the citizenship of a foreign country.
Provided that if such person's mother and father both are citizens of Nepal at the time of acquisition of citizenship, such person born in Nepal may acquire the citizenship of Nepal by descent.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 8
To be citizens of Nepal
In the cases other than those mentioned in this Article, the Government of Nepal may, in accordance with the Federal law, grant the naturalized citizenship of Nepal.
raw_text: In the cases other than those mentioned in this Article, the Government of Nepal may, in accordance with the Federal law, grant the naturalized citizenship of Nepal.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 9
To be citizens of Nepal
The Government of Nepal may, in accordance with the Federal law, grant the honorary citizenship of Nepal.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal may, in accordance with the Federal law, grant the honorary citizenship of Nepal.

title: To be citizens of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 11
Clause 10
To be citizens of Nepal
Whenever any territory is acquired by way of merger into Nepal, a person having his or her domicile in such territory shall become a citizen of Nepal, subject to the Federal law.
raw_text: Whenever any territory is acquired by way of merger into Nepal, a person having his or her domicile in such territory shall become a citizen of Nepal, subject to the Federal law.

title: Citizenship with identity of descent and gender
text:
Part 2 Article 12
Citizenship with identity of descent and gender
A person who obtains the citizenship of Nepal by descent in  accordance with this Constitution may obtain a certificate of citizenship of Nepal with gender identity by the name of his or her mother or father.
raw_text: A person who obtains the citizenship of Nepal by descent in  accordance with this Constitution may obtain a certificate of citizenship of Nepal with gender identity by the name of his or her mother or father.

title: Acquisition, reacquisition and termination of citizenship
text:
Part 2 Article 13
Acquisition, reacquisition and termination of citizenship
Other matters relating to the acquisition, reacquisition and termination of citizenship shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other matters relating to the acquisition, reacquisition and termination of citizenship shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Power to grant non-resident Nepalese citizenship
text:
Part 2 Article 14
Power to grant non-resident Nepalese citizenship
The non-residential citizenship of Nepal may be so granted to a person who has acquired the citizenship of a foreign country, has resided in a country other than a member state of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation, and who or whose father or mother, grandfather or grandmother was previously a citizen of Nepal by decent or birth but subsequently acquired the citizenship of the foreign country that such person may enjoy economic, social and cultural rights in accordance with the Federal law.
raw_text: The non-residential citizenship of Nepal may be so granted to a person who has acquired the citizenship of a foreign country, has resided in a country other than a member state of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation, and who or whose father or mother, grandfather or grandmother was previously a citizen of Nepal by decent or birth but subsequently acquired the citizenship of the foreign country that such person may enjoy economic, social and cultural rights in accordance with the Federal law.

title: Other provisions relating to citizenship of Nepal
text:
Part 2 Article 15
Other provisions relating to citizenship of Nepal
Other matters relating to the maintenance of records setting out the identity of every citizen of Nepal and the citizenship of Nepal shall be as provided for in the Federal law
raw_text: Other matters relating to the maintenance of records setting out the identity of every citizen of Nepal and the citizenship of Nepal shall be as provided for in the Federal law

title: Right to live with dignity
text:
Part 3 Article 16
Clause 1
Right to live with dignity
Every person shall have the right to live with dignity.
raw_text: Every person shall have the right to live with dignity.

title: Right to live with dignity
text:
Part 3 Article 16
Clause 2
Right to live with dignity
No law shall be made providing for the death penalty to any one.
raw_text: No law shall be made providing for the death penalty to any one.

title: Right to freedom
text:
Part 3 Article 17
Clause 1
Right to freedom
No person shall be deprived of his or her personal liberty except in accordance with law.
raw_text: No person shall be deprived of his or her personal liberty except in accordance with law.

title: Right to freedom
text:
Part 3 Article 17
Clause 2
Right to freedom
Every citizen shall have the following freedoms:
(a) freedom of opinion and expression, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal or the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or the people of various castes, tribes, religions or communities or incite caste-based discrimination or untouchability or on any act of disrespect of labour, defamation, contempt of court, incitement to an offence or on any act which may be contrary to public decency or morality.
(b) freedom to assemble peaceably and without arms, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal or the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or public peace and order.
(c) freedom to form political parties, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal, constitute an espionage against the nation or divulge national secrecy or on any act of rendering assistance to any foreign state, organization or representative in a manner to undermine the security of Nepal or on an act of sedition or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or on any act of incitement to caste-based or communal hatred or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between various castes, tribes, religions and communities, or on any act of acquisition of, or restriction on, membership of any political party on the basis solely of tribe, language, religion, community or sex or on any act of formation of a political party with discrimination between citizens or on incitement to violent acts or on any act which may be contrary to public morality.
(d) freedom to form unions and associations, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal, or on any act which may constitute espionage against the nation or on any act of divulgence of national secrecy or on any act assisting any foreign state, organization or representative in a manner to undermine the security of Nepal or on an act of sedition or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or on any act of incitement to caste-based or communal hatred or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between various castes, tribes, religions and communities or on incitement to violent acts or on any act which may be contrary to public morality.
(e) freedom to move and reside in any part of Nepal, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the interest of the general public or which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or the harmonious relations between the peoples of various castes, tribes, religions or communities or which may constitute or incite violent acts.
(f) freedom to practice any profession, carry on any occupation, and establish and operate any industry, trade and business in any part of Nepal. Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to prevent any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or any act which may be contrary to public health, decency or morality of the general public or to confer on the State the exclusive right to undertake any specific industry, trade or service, or to prescribe any condition or qualification for carrying on any industry, trade, occupation, employment or business.
raw_text:
Every citizen shall have the following freedoms:
(a) freedom of opinion and expression, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal or the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or the people of various castes, tribes, religions or communities or incite caste-based discrimination or untouchability or on any act of disrespect of labour, defamation, contempt of court, incitement to an offence or on any act which may be contrary to public decency or morality.
(b) freedom to assemble peaceably and without arms, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal or the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or public peace and order.
(c) freedom to form political parties, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal, constitute an espionage against the nation or divulge national secrecy or on any act of rendering assistance to any foreign state, organization or representative in a manner to undermine the security of Nepal or on an act of sedition or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or on any act of incitement to caste-based or communal hatred or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between various castes, tribes, religions and communities, or on any act of acquisition of, or restriction on, membership of any political party on the basis solely of tribe, language, religion, community or sex or on any act of formation of a political party with discrimination between citizens or on incitement to violent acts or on any act which may be contrary to public morality.
(d) freedom to form unions and associations, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality and independence of Nepal, or on any act which may constitute espionage against the nation or on any act of divulgence of national secrecy or on any act assisting any foreign state, organization or representative in a manner to undermine the security of Nepal or on an act of sedition or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or on any act of incitement to caste-based or communal hatred or on any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between various castes, tribes, religions and communities or on incitement to violent acts or on any act which may be contrary to public morality.
(e) freedom to move and reside in any part of Nepal, Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the interest of the general public or which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or the harmonious relations between the peoples of various castes, tribes, religions or communities or which may constitute or incite violent acts.
(f) freedom to practice any profession, carry on any occupation, and establish and operate any industry, trade and business in any part of Nepal. Provided that Nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to prevent any act which may undermine the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or any act which may be contrary to public health, decency or morality of the general public or to confer on the State the exclusive right to undertake any specific industry, trade or service, or to prescribe any condition or qualification for carrying on any industry, trade, occupation, employment or business.

title: Right to equality
text:
Part 3 Article 18
Clause 1
Right to equality
All citizens shall be equal before law. No person shall be denied the equal protection of law.
raw_text: All citizens shall be equal before law. No person shall be denied the equal protection of law.

title: Right to equality
text:
Part 3 Article 18
Clause 2
Right to equality
No discrimination shall be made in the application of general laws on grounds of origin, religion, race, caste, tribe, sex, physical condition, condition of health,  marital status, pregnancy, economic condition, language or region, ideology or on similar other grounds.
raw_text: No discrimination shall be made in the application of general laws on grounds of origin, religion, race, caste, tribe, sex, physical condition, condition of health,  marital status, pregnancy, economic condition, language or region, ideology or on similar other grounds.

title: Right to equality
text:
Part 3 Article 18
Clause 3
Right to equality
The State shall not discriminate citizens on grounds of origin, religion, race, caste, tribe, sex, economic condition, language, region, ideology or on similar other grounds.
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of special provisions by law for the protection, empowerment or development of the citizens including the socially or culturally backward women, Dalit, indigenous people, indigenous nationalities, Madhesi, Tharu, Muslim, oppressed class, Pichhada class, minorities, the marginalized, farmers, labours, youths, children,  senior citizens, gender and sexual minorities, persons with disabilities, persons in pregnancy, incapacitated or helpless, backward region and indigent Khas Arya. Explanation: For the purposes of this Part and Part 4, "indigent" means a person who earns income less than that specified by the Federal law.
raw_text:
The State shall not discriminate citizens on grounds of origin, religion, race, caste, tribe, sex, economic condition, language, region, ideology or on similar other grounds.
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of special provisions by law for the protection, empowerment or development of the citizens including the socially or culturally backward women, Dalit, indigenous people, indigenous nationalities, Madhesi, Tharu, Muslim, oppressed class, Pichhada class, minorities, the marginalized, farmers, labours, youths, children,  senior citizens, gender and sexual minorities, persons with disabilities, persons in pregnancy, incapacitated or helpless, backward region and indigent Khas Arya. Explanation: For the purposes of this Part and Part 4, "indigent" means a person who earns income less than that specified by the Federal law.

title: Right to equality
text:
Part 3 Article 18
Clause 4
Right to equality
No discrimination shall be made on the ground of gender with regard to remuneration and social security for the same work.
raw_text: No discrimination shall be made on the ground of gender with regard to remuneration and social security for the same work.

title: Right to equality
text:
Part 3 Article 18
Clause 5
Right to equality
All offspring shall have the equal right to the ancestral property without discrimination on the ground of gender.
raw_text: All offspring shall have the equal right to the ancestral property without discrimination on the ground of gender.

title: Right to communication
text:
Part 3 Article 19
Clause 1
Right to communication
No publication and broadcasting or dissemination or printing of any news item, editorial, feature article or other reading, audio and audio-visual material through any means whatsoever including electronic publication, broadcasting and printing shall be censored.
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of Acts to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality of Nepal or the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or the harmonious relations between various castes, tribes, religions or communities, or on any act of sedition, defamation or contempt of court or incitement to an offence, or on any act
which may be contrary to public decency or morality, on any act of hatred to labour and on any act of incitement to caste-based untouchability as well as gender discrimination.
raw_text:
No publication and broadcasting or dissemination or printing of any news item, editorial, feature article or other reading, audio and audio-visual material through any means whatsoever including electronic publication, broadcasting and printing shall be censored.
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of Acts to impose reasonable restrictions on any act which may undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality of Nepal or the harmonious relations between the Federal Units or the harmonious relations between various castes, tribes, religions or communities, or on any act of sedition, defamation or contempt of court or incitement to an offence, or on any act
which may be contrary to public decency or morality, on any act of hatred to labour and on any act of incitement to caste-based untouchability as well as gender discrimination.

title: Right to communication
text:
Part 3 Article 19
Clause 2
Right to communication
No radio, television, on-line or other form of digital or electronic equipment, press or other means of communication publishing, broadcasting or printing any news item, feature, editorial, article, information or other material shall be closed or seized nor shall registration thereof be cancelled nor shall such material be seized by the reason of publication, broadcasting or printing of such material through any audio, audio-visual or electronic equipment.
Provided that nothing contained in this clause shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to regulate radio, television, online or any other form of digital or electronic equipment, press or other means of communication.
raw_text:
No radio, television, on-line or other form of digital or electronic equipment, press or other means of communication publishing, broadcasting or printing any news item, feature, editorial, article, information or other material shall be closed or seized nor shall registration thereof be cancelled nor shall such material be seized by the reason of publication, broadcasting or printing of such material through any audio, audio-visual or electronic equipment.
Provided that nothing contained in this clause shall be deemed to prevent the making of an Act to regulate radio, television, online or any other form of digital or electronic equipment, press or other means of communication.

title: Right to communication
text:
Part 3 Article 19
Clause 3
Right to communication
No means of communication including the press, electronic broadcasting and telephone shall be interrupted except in accordance with law.
raw_text: No means of communication including the press, electronic broadcasting and telephone shall be interrupted except in accordance with law.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 1
Rights relating to justice
No person shall be detained in custody without informing him or her of the ground for his or her arrest.
raw_text: No person shall be detained in custody without informing him or her of the ground for his or her arrest.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 2
Rights relating to justice
Any person who is arrested shall have the right to consult a legal practitioner of his or her choice from the time of such arrest and to be defended by such legal practitioner. Any consultation made by such person with, and advice given by, his or her legal practitioner shall be confidential. Provided this clause shall not apply to a citizen of an enemy state.
Explanation: For the purpose of this clause, "legal practitioner" means any person who is authorized by law to represent any person in any court.
raw_text:
Any person who is arrested shall have the right to consult a legal practitioner of his or her choice from the time of such arrest and to be defended by such legal practitioner. Any consultation made by such person with, and advice given by, his or her legal practitioner shall be confidential. Provided this clause shall not apply to a citizen of an enemy state.
Explanation: For the purpose of this clause, "legal practitioner" means any person who is authorized by law to represent any person in any court.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 3
Rights relating to justice
Any person who is arrested shall be produced before the adjudicating authority within a period of twenty-four hours of such arrest, excluding the time necessary for the journey from the place of arrest to such
authority; and any such person shall not be detained in custody except on the order of such authority.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to a person held in preventive detention and to a citizen of an enemy state.
raw_text:
Any person who is arrested shall be produced before the adjudicating authority within a period of twenty-four hours of such arrest, excluding the time necessary for the journey from the place of arrest to such
authority; and any such person shall not be detained in custody except on the order of such authority.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to a person held in preventive detention and to a citizen of an enemy state.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 4
Rights relating to justice
No person shall be liable for punishment for an act which was not punishable by the law in force when the act was committed nor shall any person be subjected to a punishment greater than that prescribed by the law in force at the time of the commission of the offence.
raw_text: No person shall be liable for punishment for an act which was not punishable by the law in force when the act was committed nor shall any person be subjected to a punishment greater than that prescribed by the law in force at the time of the commission of the offence.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 5
Rights relating to justice
Every person charged with an offence shall be presumed innocent until proved guilty of the offence.
raw_text: Every person charged with an offence shall be presumed innocent until proved guilty of the offence.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 6
Rights relating to justice
No person shall be tried and punished for the same offence in a court more than once.
raw_text: No person shall be tried and punished for the same offence in a court more than once.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 7
Rights relating to justice
No person charged with an offence shall be compelled to testify against himself or herself.
raw_text: No person charged with an offence shall be compelled to testify against himself or herself.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 8
Rights relating to justice
Every person shall have the right to be informed of any proceedings taken against him or her.
raw_text: Every person shall have the right to be informed of any proceedings taken against him or her.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 9
Rights relating to justice
Every person shall have the right to a fair trial by an independent, impartial and competent court or judicial body.
raw_text: Every person shall have the right to a fair trial by an independent, impartial and competent court or judicial body.

title: Rights relating to justice
text:
Part 3 Article 20
Clause 10
Rights relating to justice
Any indigent party shall have the right to free legal aid in accordance with law.
raw_text: Any indigent party shall have the right to free legal aid in accordance with law.

title: Right of victim of crime
text:
Part 3 Article 21
Clause 1
Right of victim of crime
A victim of crime shall have the right to get information about the investigation and proceedings of a case in which he or she is the victim.
raw_text: A victim of crime shall have the right to get information about the investigation and proceedings of a case in which he or she is the victim.

title: Right of victim of crime
text:
Part 3 Article 21
Clause 2
Right of victim of crime
A victim of crime shall have the right to justice including social rehabilitation and compensation in accordance with law.
raw_text: A victim of crime shall have the right to justice including social rehabilitation and compensation in accordance with law.

title: Right against torture
text:
Part 3 Article 22
Clause 1
Right against torture
No person who is arrested or detained shall be subjected to physical or mental torture or to cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment.
raw_text: No person who is arrested or detained shall be subjected to physical or mental torture or to cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment.

title: Right against torture
text:
Part 3 Article 22
Clause 2
Right against torture
Any act mentioned in clause (1) shall be punishable by law, and any person who is the victim of such treatment shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.
raw_text: Any act mentioned in clause (1) shall be punishable by law, and any person who is the victim of such treatment shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.

title: Right against preventive detention
text:
Part 3 Article 23
Clause 1
Right against preventive detention
No person shall be held under preventive detention unless there is a sufficient ground of the existence of an immediate threat to the sovereignty, territorial integrity or public peace and order of Nepal.
raw_text: No person shall be held under preventive detention unless there is a sufficient ground of the existence of an immediate threat to the sovereignty, territorial integrity or public peace and order of Nepal.

title: Right against preventive detention
text:
Part 3 Article 23
Clause 2
Right against preventive detention
Information about the situation of a person who is held under preventive detention pursuant to clause (1) must be given immediately to his or her family members or relatives.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to a citizen of an enemy state.
raw_text:
Information about the situation of a person who is held under preventive detention pursuant to clause (1) must be given immediately to his or her family members or relatives.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to a citizen of an enemy state.

title: Right against preventive detention
text:
Part 3 Article 23
Clause 3
Right against preventive detention
If the authority making preventive detention holds any person under preventive detention contrary to law or in bad faith, the person held under preventive detention shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.
raw_text: If the authority making preventive detention holds any person under preventive detention contrary to law or in bad faith, the person held under preventive detention shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.

title: Right against untouchability and discrimination
text:
Part 3 Article 24
Clause 1
Right against untouchability and discrimination
No person shall be subjected to any form of untouchability or discrimination in any private and public places on grounds of his or her origin, caste, tribe, community, profession, occupation or physical condition.
raw_text: No person shall be subjected to any form of untouchability or discrimination in any private and public places on grounds of his or her origin, caste, tribe, community, profession, occupation or physical condition.

title: Right against untouchability and discrimination
text:
Part 3 Article 24
Clause 2
Right against untouchability and discrimination
In producing or distributing any goods, services or facilities, no person belonging to any particular caste or tribe shall be prevented from purchasing or acquiring such goods, services or facilities nor shall such goods, services or facilities be sold, distributed or provided only to the persons belonging to any particular caste or tribe.
raw_text: In producing or distributing any goods, services or facilities, no person belonging to any particular caste or tribe shall be prevented from purchasing or acquiring such goods, services or facilities nor shall such goods, services or facilities be sold, distributed or provided only to the persons belonging to any particular caste or tribe.

title: Right against untouchability and discrimination
text:
Part 3 Article 24
Clause 3
Right against untouchability and discrimination
No act purporting to demonstrate any person or community as superior or inferior on grounds of origin, caste, tribe or physical condition or justifying social discrimination on grounds of caste, tribe or untouchability or propagating ideology based on untouchability and caste based superiority or hatred or encouraging caste-based discrimination in any manner whatsoever shall be allowed.
raw_text: No act purporting to demonstrate any person or community as superior or inferior on grounds of origin, caste, tribe or physical condition or justifying social discrimination on grounds of caste, tribe or untouchability or propagating ideology based on untouchability and caste based superiority or hatred or encouraging caste-based discrimination in any manner whatsoever shall be allowed.

title: Right against untouchability and discrimination
text:
Part 3 Article 24
Clause 4
Right against untouchability and discrimination
No discrimination in any form shall be allowed at a workplace with or without making untouchability on the ground of caste.
raw_text: No discrimination in any form shall be allowed at a workplace with or without making untouchability on the ground of caste.

title: Right against untouchability and discrimination
text:
Part 3 Article 24
Clause 5
Right against untouchability and discrimination
Any act of untouchability and discrimination in any form committed in contravention of this Article shall be punishable by law as a severe social offence, and the victim of such act shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.
raw_text: Any act of untouchability and discrimination in any form committed in contravention of this Article shall be punishable by law as a severe social offence, and the victim of such act shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.

title: Right relating to property
text:
Part 3 Article 25
Clause 1
Right relating to property
Every citizen shall, subject to law, have the right to acquire, own, sell, dispose, acquire business profits from, and otherwise deal with, property.
Provided that the State may levy tax on property of a person, and tax on income of a person in accordance with the concept of progressive taxation. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "property" means any form of property including movable and immovable property, and includes an intellectual property right.
raw_text:
Every citizen shall, subject to law, have the right to acquire, own, sell, dispose, acquire business profits from, and otherwise deal with, property.
Provided that the State may levy tax on property of a person, and tax on income of a person in accordance with the concept of progressive taxation. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "property" means any form of property including movable and immovable property, and includes an intellectual property right.

title: Right relating to property
text:
Part 3 Article 25
Clause 2
Right relating to property
The State shall not, except for public interest, requisition, acquire, or otherwise create any encumbrance on, property of a person.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to any property acquired by any person illicitly.
raw_text:
The State shall not, except for public interest, requisition, acquire, or otherwise create any encumbrance on, property of a person.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to any property acquired by any person illicitly.

title: Right relating to property
text:
Part 3 Article 25
Clause 3
Right relating to property
The basis of compensation to be provided and procedures to be followed in the requisition by the State of property of any person for public interest in accordance with clause (3) shall be as provided for in the Act.
raw_text: The basis of compensation to be provided and procedures to be followed in the requisition by the State of property of any person for public interest in accordance with clause (3) shall be as provided for in the Act.

title: Right relating to property
text:
Part 3 Article 25
Clause 4
Right relating to property
The provisions of clauses (2) and (3) shall not prevent the State from making land reforms, management and regulation in accordance with law for the purposes of enhancement of product and productivity of lands, modernization and commercialization of agriculture, environment protection and planned housing and urban development.
raw_text: The provisions of clauses (2) and (3) shall not prevent the State from making land reforms, management and regulation in accordance with law for the purposes of enhancement of product and productivity of lands, modernization and commercialization of agriculture, environment protection and planned housing and urban development.

title: Right relating to property
text:
Part 3 Article 25
Clause 5
Right relating to property
Nothing shall prevent the State from using the property of any person, which it has requisitioned for public interest in accordance with clause (3), for any other public interest instead of such public interest.
raw_text: Nothing shall prevent the State from using the property of any person, which it has requisitioned for public interest in accordance with clause (3), for any other public interest instead of such public interest.

title: Right to freedom of religion
text:
Part 3 Article 26
Clause 1
Right to freedom of religion
Every person who has faith in religion shall have the freedom to profess, practice and protect his or her religion according to his or her conviction.
raw_text: Every person who has faith in religion shall have the freedom to profess, practice and protect his or her religion according to his or her conviction.

title: Right to freedom of religion
text:
Part 3 Article 26
Clause 2
Right to freedom of religion
Every religious denomination shall have the right to operate and protect its religious sites and religious Guthi (trusts).
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the regulation, by making law, of the operation and protection of religious sites and religious trusts and management of trust properties and lands.
raw_text:
Every religious denomination shall have the right to operate and protect its religious sites and religious Guthi (trusts).
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the regulation, by making law, of the operation and protection of religious sites and religious trusts and management of trust properties and lands.

title: Right to freedom of religion
text:
Part 3 Article 26
Clause 3
Right to freedom of religion
No person shall, in the exercise of the right conferred by this Article, do, or cause to be done, any act which may be contrary to public health, decency and morality or breach public peace, or convert another person from one religion to another or any act or conduct that may jeopardize other's religion.
raw_text: No person shall, in the exercise of the right conferred by this Article, do, or cause to be done, any act which may be contrary to public health, decency and morality or breach public peace, or convert another person from one religion to another or any act or conduct that may jeopardize other's religion.

title: Right to information
text:
Part 3 Article 27
Right to information
Every citizen shall have the right to demand and receive information on any matter of his or her interest or of public interest. Provided that no one shall be compelled to provide information on any matter of which confidentiality must be maintained in accordance with law.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to demand and receive information on any matter of his or her interest or of public interest. Provided that no one shall be compelled to provide information on any matter of which confidentiality must be maintained in accordance with law.

title: Right to privacy
text:
Part 3 Article 28
Right to privacy
The privacy of any person, his or her residence, property, document, data, correspondence and matters relating to his or her character shall, except in accordance with law, be inviolable.
raw_text: The privacy of any person, his or her residence, property, document, data, correspondence and matters relating to his or her character shall, except in accordance with law, be inviolable.

title: Right against exploitation
text:
Part 3 Article 29
Clause 1
Right against exploitation
Every person shall have the right against exploitation.
raw_text: Every person shall have the right against exploitation.

title: Right against exploitation
text:
Part 3 Article 29
Clause 2
Right against exploitation
No person shall be exploited in any manner on the grounds of religion, custom, tradition, usage, practice or on any other grounds.
raw_text: No person shall be exploited in any manner on the grounds of religion, custom, tradition, usage, practice or on any other grounds.

title: Right against exploitation
text:
Part 3 Article 29
Clause 3
Right against exploitation
No one shall be subjected to trafficking nor shall one be held in slavery or servitude.
raw_text: No one shall be subjected to trafficking nor shall one be held in slavery or servitude.

title: Right against exploitation
text:
Part 3 Article 29
Clause 4
Right against exploitation
No one shall be forced to work against his or her will.
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of law empowering the State to require citizens to perform compulsory service for public purposes.
raw_text:
No one shall be forced to work against his or her will.
Provided that nothing shall be deemed to prevent the making of law empowering the State to require citizens to perform compulsory service for public purposes.

title: Right against exploitation
text:
Part 3 Article 29
Clause 5
Right against exploitation
Act contrary to clauses (3) and (4) shall be punishable by law and the victim shall have the right to obtain compensation from the perpetrator in accordance with law.
raw_text: Act contrary to clauses (3) and (4) shall be punishable by law and the victim shall have the right to obtain compensation from the perpetrator in accordance with law.

title: Right to clean environment
text:
Part 3 Article 30
Clause 1
Right to clean environment
Every citizen shall have the right to live in a clean and healthy environment.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to live in a clean and healthy environment.

title: Right to clean environment
text:
Part 3 Article 30
Clause 2
Right to clean environment
The victim shall have the right to obtain compensation, in accordance with law, for any injury caused from environmental pollution or degradation.
raw_text: The victim shall have the right to obtain compensation, in accordance with law, for any injury caused from environmental pollution or degradation.

title: Right to clean environment
text:
Part 3 Article 30
Clause 3
Right to clean environment
This Article shall not be deemed to prevent the making of necessary legal provisions for a proper balance between the environment and development, in development works of the nation.
raw_text: This Article shall not be deemed to prevent the making of necessary legal provisions for a proper balance between the environment and development, in development works of the nation.

title: Right relating to education
text:
Part 3 Article 31
Clause 1
Right relating to education
Every citizen shall have the right of access to basic education.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right of access to basic education.

title: Right relating to education
text:
Part 3 Article 31
Clause 2
Right relating to education
Every citizen shall have the right to get compulsory and free education up to the basic level and free education up to the secondary level from the State.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to get compulsory and free education up to the basic level and free education up to the secondary level from the State.

title: Right relating to education
text:
Part 3 Article 31
Clause 3
Right relating to education
The citizens with disabilities and the economically indigent citizens shall have the right to get free higher education in accordance with law.
raw_text: The citizens with disabilities and the economically indigent citizens shall have the right to get free higher education in accordance with law.

title: Right relating to education
text:
Part 3 Article 31
Clause 4
Right relating to education
The visually impaired citizens shall have the right to get free education through brail script and the citizens with hearing or speaking impairment, to get free education through sign language, in accordance with law.
raw_text: The visually impaired citizens shall have the right to get free education through brail script and the citizens with hearing or speaking impairment, to get free education through sign language, in accordance with law.

title: Right relating to education
text:
Part 3 Article 31
Clause 5
Right relating to education
Every Nepalese community residing in Nepal shall have the right to get education in its mother tongue and, for that purpose, to open and operate schools and educational institutes, in accordance with law.
raw_text: Every Nepalese community residing in Nepal shall have the right to get education in its mother tongue and, for that purpose, to open and operate schools and educational institutes, in accordance with law.

title: Right to language and culture
text:
Part 3 Article 32
Clause 1
Right to language and culture
Every person and community shall have the right to use their languages.
raw_text: Every person and community shall have the right to use their languages.

title: Right to language and culture
text:
Part 3 Article 32
Clause 2
Right to language and culture
Every person and community shall have the right to participate in the cultural life of their communities.
raw_text: Every person and community shall have the right to participate in the cultural life of their communities.

title: Right to language and culture
text:
Part 3 Article 32
Clause 3
Right to language and culture
Every Nepalese community residing in Nepal shall have the right to preserve and promote its language, script, culture, cultural civilization and heritage.
raw_text: Every Nepalese community residing in Nepal shall have the right to preserve and promote its language, script, culture, cultural civilization and heritage.

title: Right to employment
text:
Part 3 Article 33
Clause 1
Right to employment
Every citizen shall have the right to employment. The terms and conditions of employment, and unemployment benefit shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to employment. The terms and conditions of employment, and unemployment benefit shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Right to employment
text:
Part 3 Article 33
Clause 2
Right to employment
Every citizen shall have the right to choose employment.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to choose employment.

title: Right to labour
text:
Part 3 Article 34
Clause 1
Right to labour
Every labourer shall have the right to practice appropriate labour.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "labourer" means a labourer or worker who does physical or mental work for an employer in consideration for remuneration.
raw_text:
Every labourer shall have the right to practice appropriate labour.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "labourer" means a labourer or worker who does physical or mental work for an employer in consideration for remuneration.

title: Right to labour
text:
Part 3 Article 34
Clause 2
Right to labour
Every labourer shall have the right to appropriate remuneration, facilities and contributory social security.
raw_text: Every labourer shall have the right to appropriate remuneration, facilities and contributory social security.

title: Right to labour
text:
Part 3 Article 34
Clause 3
Right to labour
Every labourer shall have the right to form and join trade unions and to engage in collective bargaining, in accordance with law.
raw_text: Every labourer shall have the right to form and join trade unions and to engage in collective bargaining, in accordance with law.

title: Right relating to health
text:
Part 3 Article 35
Clause 1
Right relating to health
Every citizen shall have the right to free basic health services from the State, and no one shall be deprived of emergency health services.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to free basic health services from the State, and no one shall be deprived of emergency health services.

title: Right relating to health
text:
Part 3 Article 35
Clause 2
Right relating to health
Every person shall have the right to get information about his or her medical treatment.
raw_text: Every person shall have the right to get information about his or her medical treatment.

title: Right relating to health
text:
Part 3 Article 35
Clause 3
Right relating to health
Every citizen shall have equal access to health services.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have equal access to health services.

title: Right relating to health
text:
Part 3 Article 35
Clause 4
Right relating to health
Every citizen shall have the right of access to clean drinking water and sanitation.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right of access to clean drinking water and sanitation.

title: Right relating to food
text:
Part 3 Article 36
Clause 1
Right relating to food
Every citizen shall have the right relating to food.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right relating to food.

title: Right relating to food
text:
Part 3 Article 36
Clause 2
Right relating to food
Every citizen shall have the right to be safe from the state of being in danger of life from the scarcity of food.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to be safe from the state of being in danger of life from the scarcity of food.

title: Right relating to food
text:
Part 3 Article 36
Clause 3
Right relating to food
Every citizen shall have the right to food sovereignty in accordance with law.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to food sovereignty in accordance with law.

title: Right to housing
text:
Part 3 Article 37
Clause 1
Right to housing
Every citizen shall have the right to an appropriate housing.
raw_text: Every citizen shall have the right to an appropriate housing.

title: Right to housing
text:
Part 3 Article 37
Clause 2
Right to housing
No citizen shall be evicted from the residence owned by him or her nor shall his or her residence be infringed except in accordance with law.
raw_text: No citizen shall be evicted from the residence owned by him or her nor shall his or her residence be infringed except in accordance with law.

title: Rights of women
text:
Part 3 Article 38
Clause 1
Rights of women
Every woman shall have equal lineage right without gender based discrimination.
raw_text: Every woman shall have equal lineage right without gender based discrimination.

title: Rights of women
text:
Part 3 Article 38
Clause 2
Rights of women
Every woman shall have the right to safe motherhood and reproductive health.
raw_text: Every woman shall have the right to safe motherhood and reproductive health.

title: Rights of women
text:
Part 3 Article 38
Clause 3
Rights of women
No woman shall be subjected to physical, mental, sexual, psychological or other form of violence or exploitation on grounds of religion, social, cultural tradition, practice or on any other grounds. Such act shall be punishable by law, and the victim shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.
raw_text: No woman shall be subjected to physical, mental, sexual, psychological or other form of violence or exploitation on grounds of religion, social, cultural tradition, practice or on any other grounds. Such act shall be punishable by law, and the victim shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.

title: Rights of women
text:
Part 3 Article 38
Clause 4
Rights of women
Women shall have the right to participate in all bodies of the State on the basis of the principle of proportional inclusion.
raw_text: Women shall have the right to participate in all bodies of the State on the basis of the principle of proportional inclusion.

title: Rights of women
text:
Part 3 Article 38
Clause 5
Rights of women
Women shall have the right to obtain special opportunity in education, health, employment and social security, on the basis of positive discrimination.
raw_text: Women shall have the right to obtain special opportunity in education, health, employment and social security, on the basis of positive discrimination.

title: Rights of women
text:
Part 3 Article 38
Clause 6
Rights of women
The spouse shall have the equal right to property and family affairs.
raw_text: The spouse shall have the equal right to property and family affairs.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 1
Rights of the child
Every child shall have the right to name and birth registration along with his or her identity.
raw_text: Every child shall have the right to name and birth registration along with his or her identity.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 2
Rights of the child
Every child shall have the right to education, health, maintenance, proper care, sports, entertainment and overall personality development from the families and the State.
raw_text: Every child shall have the right to education, health, maintenance, proper care, sports, entertainment and overall personality development from the families and the State.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 3
Rights of the child
Every child shall have the right to elementary child development and child participation.
raw_text: Every child shall have the right to elementary child development and child participation.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 4
Rights of the child
No child shall be employed to work in any factory, mine or engaged in similar other hazardous work.
raw_text: No child shall be employed to work in any factory, mine or engaged in similar other hazardous work.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 5
Rights of the child
No child shall be subjected to child marriage, transported illegally, abducted/kidnapped or taken in hostage.
raw_text: No child shall be subjected to child marriage, transported illegally, abducted/kidnapped or taken in hostage.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 6
Rights of the child
No child shall be recruited or used in army, police or any armed group, or be subjected, in the name of cultural or religious traditions, to abuse,
exclusion or physical, mental, sexual or other form of exploitation or improper use by any means or in any manner.
raw_text:
No child shall be recruited or used in army, police or any armed group, or be subjected, in the name of cultural or religious traditions, to abuse,
exclusion or physical, mental, sexual or other form of exploitation or improper use by any means or in any manner.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 7
Rights of the child
No child shall be subjected to physical, mental or any other form of torture in home, school or other place and situation whatsoever.
raw_text: No child shall be subjected to physical, mental or any other form of torture in home, school or other place and situation whatsoever.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 8
Rights of the child
Every child shall have the right to juvenile friendly justice.
raw_text: Every child shall have the right to juvenile friendly justice.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 9
Rights of the child
The child who is helpless, orphan, with disabilities, conflict victim, displaced or vulnerable shall have the right to special protection and facilities from the State.
raw_text: The child who is helpless, orphan, with disabilities, conflict victim, displaced or vulnerable shall have the right to special protection and facilities from the State.

title: Rights of the child
text:
Part 3 Article 39
Clause 10
Rights of the child
Any act contrary to in clauses (4), (5), (6) and (7) shall be punishable by law, and a child who is the victim of such act shall have the right to obtain compensation from the perpetrator, in accordance with law.
raw_text: Any act contrary to in clauses (4), (5), (6) and (7) shall be punishable by law, and a child who is the victim of such act shall have the right to obtain compensation from the perpetrator, in accordance with law.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 1
Rights of Dalit
The Dalit shall have the right to participate in all bodies of the State on the basis of the principle of proportional inclusion. Special provision shall be made by law for the empowerment, representation and participation of the Dalit community in public services as well as other sectors of employment.
raw_text: The Dalit shall have the right to participate in all bodies of the State on the basis of the principle of proportional inclusion. Special provision shall be made by law for the empowerment, representation and participation of the Dalit community in public services as well as other sectors of employment.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 2
Rights of Dalit
Provision of free education with scholarship, from primary to higher education, shall be made by law for the Dalit students. Special provision shall be made by law for the Dalit in technical and vocational education.
raw_text: Provision of free education with scholarship, from primary to higher education, shall be made by law for the Dalit students. Special provision shall be made by law for the Dalit in technical and vocational education.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 3
Rights of Dalit
Special provision shall be made by law in order to provide health and social security to the Dalit community.
raw_text: Special provision shall be made by law in order to provide health and social security to the Dalit community.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 4
Rights of Dalit
The Dalit community shall have the right to use, protect and develop their traditional occupation, knowledge, skill and technology. The State shall accord priority to the Dalit community in modern business related with their traditional occupation and provide skills and resources required therefor.
raw_text: The Dalit community shall have the right to use, protect and develop their traditional occupation, knowledge, skill and technology. The State shall accord priority to the Dalit community in modern business related with their traditional occupation and provide skills and resources required therefor.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 5
Rights of Dalit
The State shall once provide land to the landless Dalit in accordance with law.
raw_text: The State shall once provide land to the landless Dalit in accordance with law.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 6
Rights of Dalit
The State shall, in accordance with law, arrange settlement for the Dalit who do not have housing.
raw_text: The State shall, in accordance with law, arrange settlement for the Dalit who do not have housing.

title: Rights of Dalit
text:
Part 3 Article 40
Clause 7
Rights of Dalit
The facilities conferred by this Article to the Dalit community must be distributed in a just manner so that the Dalit women, men and Dalit in all communities can obtain such facilities proportionately.
raw_text: The facilities conferred by this Article to the Dalit community must be distributed in a just manner so that the Dalit women, men and Dalit in all communities can obtain such facilities proportionately.

title: Rights of senior citizens
text:
Part 3 Article 41
Rights of senior citizens
The senior citizens shall have the right to special protection and social security from the State.
raw_text: The senior citizens shall have the right to special protection and social security from the State.

title: Right to social justice
text:
Part 3 Article 42
Clause 1
Right to social justice
The socially backward women, Dalit, indigenous people, indigenous nationalities, Madhesi, Tharu, minorities, persons with disabilities, marginalized communities, Muslims, backward classes, gender and sexual minorities, youths, farmers, labourers, oppressed or citizens of backward regions and indigent Khas Arya shall have the right to participate in the State bodies on the basis of inclusive principle.
raw_text: The socially backward women, Dalit, indigenous people, indigenous nationalities, Madhesi, Tharu, minorities, persons with disabilities, marginalized communities, Muslims, backward classes, gender and sexual minorities, youths, farmers, labourers, oppressed or citizens of backward regions and indigent Khas Arya shall have the right to participate in the State bodies on the basis of inclusive principle.

title: Right to social justice
text:
Part 3 Article 42
Clause 2
Right to social justice
The indigent citizens and citizens of the communities on the verge of extinction shall have the right to get special opportunities and benefits in education, health, housing, employment, food and social security for their protection, upliftment, empowerment and development.
raw_text: The indigent citizens and citizens of the communities on the verge of extinction shall have the right to get special opportunities and benefits in education, health, housing, employment, food and social security for their protection, upliftment, empowerment and development.

title: Right to social justice
text:
Part 3 Article 42
Clause 3
Right to social justice
The citizens with disabilities shall have the right to live with dignity and honour, with the identity of their diversity, and have equal access to public services and facilities.
raw_text: The citizens with disabilities shall have the right to live with dignity and honour, with the identity of their diversity, and have equal access to public services and facilities.

title: Right to social justice
text:
Part 3 Article 42
Clause 4
Right to social justice
Every farmer shall have the right to have access to lands for agro activities, select and protect local seeds and agro species which have been used and pursued traditionally, in accordance with law.
raw_text: Every farmer shall have the right to have access to lands for agro activities, select and protect local seeds and agro species which have been used and pursued traditionally, in accordance with law.

title: Right to social justice
text:
Part 3 Article 42
Clause 5
Right to social justice
The families of the martyrs who have sacrificed their life, persons who were forced to disappear, and those who became disabled and
injured in all popular movements, armed conflicts and revolutions that have been carried out for progressive democratic changes in Nepal, democracy fighters, conflict victims and displaced ones, persons with disabilities, the injured and victims shall have the right to get a  prioritized opportunity, with justice and due respect, in education, health, employment, housing and social security, in accordance with law.
raw_text:
The families of the martyrs who have sacrificed their life, persons who were forced to disappear, and those who became disabled and
injured in all popular movements, armed conflicts and revolutions that have been carried out for progressive democratic changes in Nepal, democracy fighters, conflict victims and displaced ones, persons with disabilities, the injured and victims shall have the right to get a  prioritized opportunity, with justice and due respect, in education, health, employment, housing and social security, in accordance with law.

title: Right to social security
text:
Part 3 Article 43
Right to social security
The indigent citizens, incapacitated and helpless citizens, helpless single women, citizens with disabilities, children, citizens who cannot take care themselves and citizens belonging to the tribes on the verge of extinction shall have the right to social security, in accordance with law.
raw_text: The indigent citizens, incapacitated and helpless citizens, helpless single women, citizens with disabilities, children, citizens who cannot take care themselves and citizens belonging to the tribes on the verge of extinction shall have the right to social security, in accordance with law.

title: Rights of the consumer
text:
Part 3 Article 44
Clause 1
Rights of the consumer
Every consumer shall have the right to obtain quality goods and services.
raw_text: Every consumer shall have the right to obtain quality goods and services.

title: Rights of the consumer
text:
Part 3 Article 44
Clause 2
Rights of the consumer
A person who has suffered injury from any substandard goods or services shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.
raw_text: A person who has suffered injury from any substandard goods or services shall have the right to obtain compensation in accordance with law.

title: Right against exile
text:
Part 3 Article 45
Right against exile
No citizen shall be exiled.
raw_text: No citizen shall be exiled.

title: Right to constitutional remedies
text:
Part 3 Article 46
Right to constitutional remedies
There shall be a right to obtain constitutional remedies in the manner set forth in Article 133 or 144 for the enforcement of the rights conferred by this Part.
raw_text: There shall be a right to obtain constitutional remedies in the manner set forth in Article 133 or 144 for the enforcement of the rights conferred by this Part.

title: Implementation of fundamental rights
text:
Part 3 Article 47
Implementation of fundamental rights
The State shall, as required, make legal provisions for the implementation of the rights conferred by this Part, within three years of the commencement of this Constitution.
raw_text: The State shall, as required, make legal provisions for the implementation of the rights conferred by this Part, within three years of the commencement of this Constitution.

title: Duties of citizens
text:
Part 3 Article 48
Duties of citizens
Every citizen shall have the following duties: (a) to safeguard the nationality, sovereignty and integrity of Nepal, while being loyal to the nation, (b) to abide by the Constitution and law, (c) to render compulsory service as and when the State so requires,
(d) to protect and preserve public property
raw_text:
Every citizen shall have the following duties: (a) to safeguard the nationality, sovereignty and integrity of Nepal, while being loyal to the nation, (b) to abide by the Constitution and law, (c) to render compulsory service as and when the State so requires,
(d) to protect and preserve public property

title: To be guiding principles
text:
Part 4 Article 49
Clause 1
To be guiding principles
The directive principles, policies and obligations set forth in this Part shall be the guiding principles for the governance of the State.
raw_text: The directive principles, policies and obligations set forth in this Part shall be the guiding principles for the governance of the State.

title: To be guiding principles
text:
Part 4 Article 49
Clause 2
To be guiding principles
The State shall mobilize, or cause to be mobilized, means and resources, as required, to implement the principles, policies and obligations set forth in this Part.
raw_text: The State shall mobilize, or cause to be mobilized, means and resources, as required, to implement the principles, policies and obligations set forth in this Part.

title: Directive principles
text:
Part 4 Article 50
Clause 1
Directive principles
The political objective of the State shall be to establish a public welfare system of governance, by establishing a just system in all aspects of the national life through the rule of law, values and norms of fundamental rights and human rights, gender equality, proportional inclusion, participation and social justice, while at the same time protecting the life, property, equality and liberties of the people, in keeping with the vitality of freedom, sovereignty, territorial integrity and independence of Nepal, and to consolidate a federal democratic republican system of governance in order to ensure an atmosphere conducive to the enjoyment of the fruits of democracy, while at the same time maintaining the relations between the Federal Units on the basis of cooperative federalism and incorporating the principle of proportional participation in the system of governance on the basis of local autonomy and decentralization.
raw_text: The political objective of the State shall be to establish a public welfare system of governance, by establishing a just system in all aspects of the national life through the rule of law, values and norms of fundamental rights and human rights, gender equality, proportional inclusion, participation and social justice, while at the same time protecting the life, property, equality and liberties of the people, in keeping with the vitality of freedom, sovereignty, territorial integrity and independence of Nepal, and to consolidate a federal democratic republican system of governance in order to ensure an atmosphere conducive to the enjoyment of the fruits of democracy, while at the same time maintaining the relations between the Federal Units on the basis of cooperative federalism and incorporating the principle of proportional participation in the system of governance on the basis of local autonomy and decentralization.

title: Directive principles
text:
Part 4 Article 50
Clause 2
Directive principles
The social and cultural objective of the State shall be to build a civilized and egalitarian society by eliminating all forms of discrimination, exploitation and injustice on the grounds of religion, culture, tradition, usage, custom, practice or on any other similar grounds, to develop social, cultural values founded on national pride, democracy, pro-people, respect of labour, entrepreneurship, discipline, dignity and harmony, and to consolidate the
