raw_text: The annual estimate to be laid pursuant to clause (1) shall also be accompanied by a statement of the expenses allocated to every Ministry in the previous financial year and particulars of whether the objectives of the expenses have been achieved.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text:
Part 10 Article 119
Clause 3
Estimates of revenues and expenditures
The Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal shall lay before the Federal Parliament an estimate of revenues and expenditures under clause (1) on the 15th day of Jesta (mid May) each year.
raw_text: The Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal shall lay before the Federal Parliament an estimate of revenues and expenditures under clause (1) on the 15th day of Jesta (mid May) each year.

title: Appropriation Act
text:
Part 10 Article 120
Appropriation Act
The moneys to be spent under an Appropriation Act shall be specified under concerned heads in an Appropriation Bill.
raw_text: The moneys to be spent under an Appropriation Act shall be specified under concerned heads in an Appropriation Bill.

title: Supplementary estimates
text:
Part 10 Article 121
Clause 1
Supplementary estimates
The Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal may lay before the House of Representatives a supplementary estimate if it is found in any financial year,-
(a) that the sum authorized to be spent for a particular service by the Appropriation Act for the current financial year is insufficient, or that a need has arisen for expenditures on some new service not provided for by the Appropriation Act for that year, or
(b) that the expenditures made during that financial year are in excess of the amount authorized by the Appropriation Act.
raw_text:
The Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal may lay before the House of Representatives a supplementary estimate if it is found in any financial year,-
(a) that the sum authorized to be spent for a particular service by the Appropriation Act for the current financial year is insufficient, or that a need has arisen for expenditures on some new service not provided for by the Appropriation Act for that year, or
(b) that the expenditures made during that financial year are in excess of the amount authorized by the Appropriation Act.

title: Supplementary estimates
text:
Part 10 Article 121
Clause 2
Supplementary estimates
The sums included in the supplementary estimate shall be specified under the related heads in a Supplementary Appropriation Bill.
raw_text: The sums included in the supplementary estimate shall be specified under the related heads in a Supplementary Appropriation Bill.

title: Votes on account
text:
Part 10 Article 122
Clause 1
Votes on account
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, a portion of the expenditure estimated for the financial year may, when an Appropriation Bill is under consideration, be incurred in advance, as provided for in the Federal Act.
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, a portion of the expenditure estimated for the financial year may, when an Appropriation Bill is under consideration, be incurred in advance, as provided for in the Federal Act.

title: Votes on account
text:
Part 10 Article 122
Clause 2
Votes on account
A Vote on Account Bill shall not be introduced until the estimates of revenues and expenditures have been laid in accordance with Article 119, and the sums involved in the Vote on Account shall not exceed one-third of the estimate of expenditures for the financial year.
raw_text: A Vote on Account Bill shall not be introduced until the estimates of revenues and expenditures have been laid in accordance with Article 119, and the sums involved in the Vote on Account shall not exceed one-third of the estimate of expenditures for the financial year.

title: Votes on account
text:
Part 10 Article 122
Clause 3
Votes on account
The expenditures incurred in accordance with the Federal Vote on Account Act shall be included in the Appropriation Bill.
raw_text: The expenditures incurred in accordance with the Federal Vote on Account Act shall be included in the Appropriation Bill.

title: Votes of credit
text:
Part 10 Article 123
Votes of credit
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, if owing to an emergency due to either natural causes or a threat of external aggression or internal disturbances or other reasons, it appears to be impractical or inexpedient in view of the security or interest of the country to specify the details required under Article 119, the Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal may lay before the House of Representatives a Vote of Credit Bill giving only a statement of expenditures.
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, if owing to an emergency due to either natural causes or a threat of external aggression or internal disturbances or other reasons, it appears to be impractical or inexpedient in view of the security or interest of the country to specify the details required under Article 119, the Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal may lay before the House of Representatives a Vote of Credit Bill giving only a statement of expenditures.

title: Federal contingency fund
text:
Part 10 Article 124
Clause 1
Federal contingency fund
A Federal Act may create a fund to be known as the contingency fund into which such moneys as may be determined by the Federal Act shall be paid from time to time.
raw_text: A Federal Act may create a fund to be known as the contingency fund into which such moneys as may be determined by the Federal Act shall be paid from time to time.

title: Federal contingency fund
text:
Part 10 Article 124
Clause 2
Federal contingency fund
The Government of Nepal shall control the fund under clause (1), and may meet any unforeseen expenditure out of such fund.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal shall control the fund under clause (1), and may meet any unforeseen expenditure out of such fund.

title: Federal contingency fund
text:
Part 10 Article 124
Clause 3
Federal contingency fund
The amount of the expenditure under clause (2) shall be reimbursed as soon as possible as provided for in the Federal Act.
raw_text: The amount of the expenditure under clause (2) shall be reimbursed as soon as possible as provided for in the Federal Act.

title: Act relating to financial procedures
text:
Part 10 Article 125
Act relating to financial procedures
Matters relating to the transfer of moneys appropriated by the Federal Act from one head to another and other financial procedures shall be as provided for in the Federal Act
raw_text: Matters relating to the transfer of moneys appropriated by the Federal Act from one head to another and other financial procedures shall be as provided for in the Federal Act

title: Courts to exercise powers relating to justice
text:
Part 11 Article 126
Clause 1
Courts to exercise powers relating to justice
Powers relating to justice in Nepal shall be exercised by courts and other judicial bodies in accordance with this Constitution, other laws and the recognized principles of justice.
raw_text: Powers relating to justice in Nepal shall be exercised by courts and other judicial bodies in accordance with this Constitution, other laws and the recognized principles of justice.

title: Courts to exercise powers relating to justice
text:
Part 11 Article 126
Clause 2
Courts to exercise powers relating to justice
All shall abide by the orders or decisions made in the course of trial of lawsuits by the courts.
raw_text: All shall abide by the orders or decisions made in the course of trial of lawsuits by the courts.

title: Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 127
Clause 1
Courts
There shall be the following courts in Nepal:
(a) Supreme Court,
(b) High Court, and
(c) District Court.
raw_text:
There shall be the following courts in Nepal:
(a) Supreme Court,
(b) High Court, and
(c) District Court.

title: Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 127
Clause 2
Courts
In addition to the courts under clause (1), judicial bodies may be formed at the Local level to try cases under law or other bodies as required may be formed to pursue alternative dispute settlement methods.
raw_text: In addition to the courts under clause (1), judicial bodies may be formed at the Local level to try cases under law or other bodies as required may be formed to pursue alternative dispute settlement methods.

title: Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 128
Clause 1
Supreme Court
There shall be a Supreme Court in Nepal.
raw_text: There shall be a Supreme Court in Nepal.

title: Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 128
Clause 2
Supreme Court
The Supreme Court shall be a court of record. All courts and judicial bodies shall, except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, be under the Supreme Court. The Supreme Court shall have the final authority to interpret this Constitution and laws.
raw_text: The Supreme Court shall be a court of record. All courts and judicial bodies shall, except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, be under the Supreme Court. The Supreme Court shall have the final authority to interpret this Constitution and laws.

title: Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 128
Clause 3
Supreme Court
The Supreme Court may inspect, supervise and give necessary directives to, it and courts, specialized courts or other judicial bodies under its jurisdiction, in relation to matters relating to judicial administration or management.
raw_text: The Supreme Court may inspect, supervise and give necessary directives to, it and courts, specialized courts or other judicial bodies under its jurisdiction, in relation to matters relating to judicial administration or management.

title: Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 128
Clause 4
Supreme Court
All must abide by any interpretation of the Constitution or a law made by or any legal principle laid down by the Supreme Court in the course of trying a lawsuit. If anyone makes obstruction in the dispensation of justice
by, or disregard any order or judgment handed down by, it or any of its subordinate courts, the Supreme Court may, in accordance with law, initiate proceedings and impose punishment for contempt.
raw_text:
All must abide by any interpretation of the Constitution or a law made by or any legal principle laid down by the Supreme Court in the course of trying a lawsuit. If anyone makes obstruction in the dispensation of justice
by, or disregard any order or judgment handed down by, it or any of its subordinate courts, the Supreme Court may, in accordance with law, initiate proceedings and impose punishment for contempt.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 129
Clause 1
Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
The Supreme Court shall consist of a maximum of twenty Judges, in addition to the Chief Justice of Nepal.
raw_text: The Supreme Court shall consist of a maximum of twenty Judges, in addition to the Chief Justice of Nepal.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 129
Clause 2
Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
The President shall appoint the Chief Justice, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, and other Judges of the Supreme Court, on recommendation of the Judicial Council.
raw_text: The President shall appoint the Chief Justice, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, and other Judges of the Supreme Court, on recommendation of the Judicial Council.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 129
Clause 3
Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
Any person who has served as a Judge of the Supreme Court for at least three years shall be qualified for appointment as the Chief Justice.
raw_text: Any person who has served as a Judge of the Supreme Court for at least three years shall be qualified for appointment as the Chief Justice.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 129
Clause 4
Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
The term of office of the Chief Justice shall be six years.
raw_text: The term of office of the Chief Justice shall be six years.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 129
Clause 5
Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
Any citizen of Nepal who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and served as the Chief Judge or a Judge of a High Court for at least five years or who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and constantly practiced law as a senior advocate or advocate for at least fifteen years or who is a distinguished jurist having constantly worked for at least fifteen years in the judicial or legal field or who has served in the post of Gazetted first class or a higher post of the Judicial Service for at least twelve years shall be deemed qualified for appointment as a Judge of the Supreme Court.
Explanation: The period during which one has served as the Chief Judge or Judge of an Appellate Court prior to the commencement of this Constitution shall, for the purposes of this clause, be deemed to be the period of service in the capacity of the Chief Judge or Judge of the High Court.
raw_text:
Any citizen of Nepal who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and served as the Chief Judge or a Judge of a High Court for at least five years or who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and constantly practiced law as a senior advocate or advocate for at least fifteen years or who is a distinguished jurist having constantly worked for at least fifteen years in the judicial or legal field or who has served in the post of Gazetted first class or a higher post of the Judicial Service for at least twelve years shall be deemed qualified for appointment as a Judge of the Supreme Court.
Explanation: The period during which one has served as the Chief Judge or Judge of an Appellate Court prior to the commencement of this Constitution shall, for the purposes of this clause, be deemed to be the period of service in the capacity of the Chief Judge or Judge of the High Court.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 129
Clause 6
Appointment and qualification of Chief Justice of Nepal and Judges of Supreme Court
If the office of the Chief Justice falls vacant or the Chief Justice is unable to carry out the duties of his or her office by reason of illness or otherwise or he or she cannot be present in the Supreme Court by reason of a
leave of absence or his or her being outside of Nepal, the senior-most Judge of the Supreme Court shall act as the Acting Chief Justice.
raw_text:
If the office of the Chief Justice falls vacant or the Chief Justice is unable to carry out the duties of his or her office by reason of illness or otherwise or he or she cannot be present in the Supreme Court by reason of a
leave of absence or his or her being outside of Nepal, the senior-most Judge of the Supreme Court shall act as the Acting Chief Justice.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 130
Clause 1
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
If the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court who has served for at least five years resigns or undergoes compulsory retirement or dies, he or she shall be entitled to such pension as may be provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: If the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court who has served for at least five years resigns or undergoes compulsory retirement or dies, he or she shall be entitled to such pension as may be provided for in the Federal law.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 130
Clause 2
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
Except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, the remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court shall be provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, the remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court shall be provided for in the Federal law.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 130
Clause 3
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
Notwithstanding anything contained in clauses (1) and (2), the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court who has been removed from office by way of impeachment or who has been punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude shall not be entitled to gratuity or pension.
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clauses (1) and (2), the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court who has been removed from office by way of impeachment or who has been punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude shall not be entitled to gratuity or pension.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 130
Clause 4
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Justice and Judges
The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court shall not be altered to his or her disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.
raw_text:
The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court shall not be altered to his or her disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Vacation of office of Chief Justice or Judge of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 131
Vacation of office of Chief Justice or Judge of Supreme Court
The office of the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances: (a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the President, (b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-five years, (c) if a motion of impeachment is passed against him or her under Article 101,
(d) if the President removes from office the Chief Justice, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, and the Judge of the Supreme Court, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, on the ground that he or she is unable to discharge his or her duties because of physical or mental illness, (e) if he or she is punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude, (f) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances: (a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the President, (b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-five years, (c) if a motion of impeachment is passed against him or her under Article 101,
(d) if the President removes from office the Chief Justice, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, and the Judge of the Supreme Court, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, on the ground that he or she is unable to discharge his or her duties because of physical or mental illness, (e) if he or she is punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude, (f) if he or she dies.

title: Chief Justice and Judges of Supreme Court not to be engaged in any other office
text:
Part 11 Article 132
Clause 1
Chief Justice and Judges of Supreme Court not to be engaged in any other office
No Chief Justice or Judge of the Supreme Court shall be engaged in or deputed to any office other than that of Judge.
Provided that the Government of Nepal may, in consultation with the Judicial Council, depute a Judge of the Supreme Court to work concerning judicial inquiry, or to legal or judicial investigation or research for a specified period.
raw_text:
No Chief Justice or Judge of the Supreme Court shall be engaged in or deputed to any office other than that of Judge.
Provided that the Government of Nepal may, in consultation with the Judicial Council, depute a Judge of the Supreme Court to work concerning judicial inquiry, or to legal or judicial investigation or research for a specified period.

title: Chief Justice and Judges of Supreme Court not to be engaged in any other office
text:
Part 11 Article 132
Clause 2
Chief Justice and Judges of Supreme Court not to be engaged in any other office
No person who has once held the office of Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be eligible for appointment to any government office, except as otherwise provided in this Constitution.
raw_text: No person who has once held the office of Chief Justice or a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be eligible for appointment to any government office, except as otherwise provided in this Constitution.

title: Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 133
Clause 1
Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
Any citizen of Nepal may file a petition in the Supreme Court to have any law or any part thereof declared void on the ground of inconsistency with this Constitution because it imposes an unreasonable restriction on the enjoyment of any fundamental right conferred by this Constitution or on any other ground, or to have any law or any part thereof made by a State Assembly declared void because it is inconsistent with any law made by the Federal Parliament or to have any law or any part thereof made by a Municipal Assembly or Village Assembly declared void because it is inconsistent with a law made by the Federal Parliament or the State
Assembly, and the Supreme Court shall have an extra-ordinary power to declare that law to be void either ab initio or from the date of its decision if the law appears to be so inconsistent.
raw_text:
Any citizen of Nepal may file a petition in the Supreme Court to have any law or any part thereof declared void on the ground of inconsistency with this Constitution because it imposes an unreasonable restriction on the enjoyment of any fundamental right conferred by this Constitution or on any other ground, or to have any law or any part thereof made by a State Assembly declared void because it is inconsistent with any law made by the Federal Parliament or to have any law or any part thereof made by a Municipal Assembly or Village Assembly declared void because it is inconsistent with a law made by the Federal Parliament or the State
Assembly, and the Supreme Court shall have an extra-ordinary power to declare that law to be void either ab initio or from the date of its decision if the law appears to be so inconsistent.

title: Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 133
Clause 2
Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
The Supreme Court shall, for the enforcement of the fundamental rights conferred by this Constitution or of any other legal right for which no other remedy has been provided or for which the remedy even though provided appears to be inadequate or ineffective or for the settlement of any constitutional or legal question involved in any dispute of public interest or concern, have the extraordinary power to issue necessary and appropriate orders, provide appropriate remedies, enforce such right or settle such dispute.
raw_text: The Supreme Court shall, for the enforcement of the fundamental rights conferred by this Constitution or of any other legal right for which no other remedy has been provided or for which the remedy even though provided appears to be inadequate or ineffective or for the settlement of any constitutional or legal question involved in any dispute of public interest or concern, have the extraordinary power to issue necessary and appropriate orders, provide appropriate remedies, enforce such right or settle such dispute.

title: Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 133
Clause 3
Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
Under the extra-ordinary jurisdiction under clause (2), the Supreme Court may issue appropriate orders and writs including the writs of habeas corpus, mandamus, certiorari, prohibition and quo warranto.
Provided that except on the ground of lack of jurisdiction, the Supreme Court shall not under this clause interfere with any internal proceedings of the Federal Parliament or State Assembly, and with any proceedings instituted by the Federal Parliament or State Assembly concerning violation of its privileges and penalties imposed therefor.
raw_text:
Under the extra-ordinary jurisdiction under clause (2), the Supreme Court may issue appropriate orders and writs including the writs of habeas corpus, mandamus, certiorari, prohibition and quo warranto.
Provided that except on the ground of lack of jurisdiction, the Supreme Court shall not under this clause interfere with any internal proceedings of the Federal Parliament or State Assembly, and with any proceedings instituted by the Federal Parliament or State Assembly concerning violation of its privileges and penalties imposed therefor.

title: Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 133
Clause 4
Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
Subject to this Constitution, the Supreme Court shall, as provided in a Federal law, have the power to originally try and settle cases, hear appeals, test judgments referred for confirmation, revise cases, hear petitions or review its judgments or final orders. Judges other than those having handed down the previous judgment shall make such review.
raw_text: Subject to this Constitution, the Supreme Court shall, as provided in a Federal law, have the power to originally try and settle cases, hear appeals, test judgments referred for confirmation, revise cases, hear petitions or review its judgments or final orders. Judges other than those having handed down the previous judgment shall make such review.

title: Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 133
Clause 5
Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
The Supreme Court shall settle appeals from cases originally tried and settled by a High Court and matters of public importance involving questions of interpretation of the Constitution and law or cases recommended
by a High Court, accompanied by its opinion, that it is reasonable that decision be made by the Supreme Court.
raw_text:
The Supreme Court shall settle appeals from cases originally tried and settled by a High Court and matters of public importance involving questions of interpretation of the Constitution and law or cases recommended
by a High Court, accompanied by its opinion, that it is reasonable that decision be made by the Supreme Court.

title: Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
text:
Part 11 Article 133
Clause 6
Jurisdiction of Supreme Court
Other powers and procedures of the Supreme Court shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other powers and procedures of the Supreme Court shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Power to transfer of cases
text:
Part 11 Article 134
Clause 1
Power to transfer of cases
If cases which involve substantially the same questions are sub judice before the Supreme Court and a High Court, and the Supreme Court is satisfied on its own or on application by the Attorney General or parties to such cases that such questions are of public importance, the Supreme Court shall have the power to procure and adjudge the cases together.
raw_text: If cases which involve substantially the same questions are sub judice before the Supreme Court and a High Court, and the Supreme Court is satisfied on its own or on application by the Attorney General or parties to such cases that such questions are of public importance, the Supreme Court shall have the power to procure and adjudge the cases together.

title: Power to transfer of cases
text:
Part 11 Article 134
Clause 2
Power to transfer of cases
If there exists a special situation where judicial impartiality can be questioned if a case filed in a High Court is tried by that Court, the Supreme Court may, for reasons and grounds to be recorded, order such case to be transferred from such High Court to another High Court and tried by the latter High Court, in accordance with law.
raw_text: If there exists a special situation where judicial impartiality can be questioned if a case filed in a High Court is tried by that Court, the Supreme Court may, for reasons and grounds to be recorded, order such case to be transferred from such High Court to another High Court and tried by the latter High Court, in accordance with law.

title: Not to be engaged in practice of law
text:
Part 11 Article 135
Not to be engaged in practice of law
No Chief Justice or Judge of the Supreme Court may, after retirement from service, be engaged in the practice of law, mediation or arbitration proceedings before any office or court.
raw_text: No Chief Justice or Judge of the Supreme Court may, after retirement from service, be engaged in the practice of law, mediation or arbitration proceedings before any office or court.

title: Responsibility of Chief Justice
text:
Part 11 Article 136
Responsibility of Chief Justice
The Chief Justice shall have the ultimate responsibility to make effective the administration of justice by the Supreme Court, subordinate courts, specialized courts or other judicial bodies.
raw_text: The Chief Justice shall have the ultimate responsibility to make effective the administration of justice by the Supreme Court, subordinate courts, specialized courts or other judicial bodies.

title: Formation of Constitutional Bench
text:
Part 11 Article 137
Clause 1
Formation of Constitutional Bench
There shall be a Constitutional Bench in the Supreme Court. The Constitutional Bench shall consist of the Chief Justice and other four Judges designated by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council.
raw_text: There shall be a Constitutional Bench in the Supreme Court. The Constitutional Bench shall consist of the Chief Justice and other four Judges designated by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council.

title: Formation of Constitutional Bench
text:
Part 11 Article 137
Clause 2
Formation of Constitutional Bench
The Bench under clause (1) shall originally try and settle the following cases, in addition to the petitions filed in accordance with clause (1) of Article 133:
raw_text: The Bench under clause (1) shall originally try and settle the following cases, in addition to the petitions filed in accordance with clause (1) of Article 133:

title: Formation of Constitutional Bench
text:
Part 11 Article 137
Clause 3
Formation of Constitutional Bench
Notwithstanding anything contained in Article 133, if it appears that any case sub judice in the Supreme Court involves a question of serious constitutional interpretation, the Chief Justice may appoint such case to be tried by the Bench under clause (1).
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained in Article 133, if it appears that any case sub judice in the Supreme Court involves a question of serious constitutional interpretation, the Chief Justice may appoint such case to be tried by the Bench under clause (1).

title: Formation of Constitutional Bench
text:
Part 11 Article 137
Clause 4
Formation of Constitutional Bench
Other provisions relating to the functioning of the Constitutional Bench shall be as determined by the Supreme Court.
raw_text: Other provisions relating to the functioning of the Constitutional Bench shall be as determined by the Supreme Court.

title: Annual report
text:
Part 11 Article 138
Clause 1
Annual report
The Supreme Court, Judicial Council and Judicial Service Commission shall, every year, submit annual reports to the President, and the President shall submit such reports to the Federal Parliament through the Prime Minister.
raw_text: The Supreme Court, Judicial Council and Judicial Service Commission shall, every year, submit annual reports to the President, and the President shall submit such reports to the Federal Parliament through the Prime Minister.

title: Annual report
text:
Part 11 Article 138
Clause 2
Annual report
If the Federal Parliament, upon deliberating on the annual reports submitted under clause (1), deems it necessary to give any suggestion, it may give such suggestion to the concerned body through the Government of Nepal, Ministry of Law and Justice.
raw_text: If the Federal Parliament, upon deliberating on the annual reports submitted under clause (1), deems it necessary to give any suggestion, it may give such suggestion to the concerned body through the Government of Nepal, Ministry of Law and Justice.

title: Annual report
text:
Part 11 Article 138
Clause 3
Annual report
Other provisions relating to annual reports under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other provisions relating to annual reports under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: High Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 139
Clause 1
High Courts
There shall be a High Court in each State.
raw_text: There shall be a High Court in each State.

title: High Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 139
Clause 2
High Courts
If anyone makes obstruction in the dispensation of justice by, or disregard any order or judgment handed down by, it or any of its subordinate courts or judicial bodies, the High Court may initiate proceedings on and impose punishment for contempt, as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: If anyone makes obstruction in the dispensation of justice by, or disregard any order or judgment handed down by, it or any of its subordinate courts or judicial bodies, the High Court may initiate proceedings on and impose punishment for contempt, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: High Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 139
Clause 3
High Courts
Each High Court shall consist of such number of Judges, in addition to the Chief Judge, as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Each High Court shall consist of such number of Judges, in addition to the Chief Judge, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 140
Clause 1
Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
The Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, appoint the Chief Judge and Judges of the High Court.
raw_text: The Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, appoint the Chief Judge and Judges of the High Court.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 140
Clause 2
Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
Any citizen of Nepal who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and served as a Judge of a District Court for at least five years or who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and constantly practiced law as a senior advocate or advocate for at least ten years or who has constantly been engaged in the teaching or research of law or in any other field relation to law or justice for at least ten years or who has served in the post of at least Gazetted first class of the Judicial Service for at least five years shall be deemed qualified for appointment as the Chief Judge or a Judge of the High Court.
raw_text: Any citizen of Nepal who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and served as a Judge of a District Court for at least five years or who has obtained a bachelor's degree in law and constantly practiced law as a senior advocate or advocate for at least ten years or who has constantly been engaged in the teaching or research of law or in any other field relation to law or justice for at least ten years or who has served in the post of at least Gazetted first class of the Judicial Service for at least five years shall be deemed qualified for appointment as the Chief Judge or a Judge of the High Court.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 140
Clause 3
Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
Appointments to the Chief Judge and Judges of a High Court shall be made from amongst the persons who have possessed the qualification under clause (2) on the basis of the proportion of cases settled by him or her annually and of evaluation of cases upheld, quashed or reversed by superior courts in the course of final decision thereof, in the case of a District Judge, and of his or her seniority, qualification and evaluation of standard of performance of business, in the case of a person who has served in the post of at least Gazetted first class of the Judicial Service, and of evaluation of seniority, professional continuity, honesty, professional conduct and contribution made to the field of law and justice, in the case of others.
raw_text: Appointments to the Chief Judge and Judges of a High Court shall be made from amongst the persons who have possessed the qualification under clause (2) on the basis of the proportion of cases settled by him or her annually and of evaluation of cases upheld, quashed or reversed by superior courts in the course of final decision thereof, in the case of a District Judge, and of his or her seniority, qualification and evaluation of standard of performance of business, in the case of a person who has served in the post of at least Gazetted first class of the Judicial Service, and of evaluation of seniority, professional continuity, honesty, professional conduct and contribution made to the field of law and justice, in the case of others.

title: Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 140
Clause 4
Appointment and qualification of Chief Judge and Judges of High Court
If the office of the Chief Judge falls vacant or the Chief Judge is otherwise unable to carry out the duties of his or her office or cannot be present in the High Court by reason of a leave of absence or his or her being outside of the State, the senior-most Judge of the High Court shall act as the Acting Chief Judge.
raw_text: If the office of the Chief Judge falls vacant or the Chief Judge is otherwise unable to carry out the duties of his or her office or cannot be present in the High Court by reason of a leave of absence or his or her being outside of the State, the senior-most Judge of the High Court shall act as the Acting Chief Judge.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Judge and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 141
Clause 1
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Judge and Judges
Except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, the remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Judge and Judges of the High Court shall be provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, the remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Judge and Judges of the High Court shall be provided for in the Federal law.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Judge and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 141
Clause 2
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Judge and Judges
Notwithstanding anything contained in clause (1), the Chief Judge or a Judge of a High Court who has been removed from office upon action by the Judicial Council or upon punishment by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude shall not be entitled to gratuity or pension.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where the Judicial Council has removed him or her from office on the ground of inability to perform the duties of his or her office because of physical or mental illness.
raw_text:
Notwithstanding anything contained in clause (1), the Chief Judge or a Judge of a High Court who has been removed from office upon action by the Judicial Council or upon punishment by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude shall not be entitled to gratuity or pension.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where the Judicial Council has removed him or her from office on the ground of inability to perform the duties of his or her office because of physical or mental illness.

title: Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Judge and Judges
text:
Part 11 Article 141
Clause 3
Conditions of service and facilities of Chief Judge and Judges
The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Judge or a Judge of the High Court shall not be altered to his or her disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.
raw_text:
The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Judge or a Judge of the High Court shall not be altered to his or her disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Vacation of office of Chief Judge or Judge
text:
Part 11 Article 142
Clause 1
Vacation of office of Chief Judge or Judge
The office of Chief Judge or Judge of a High Court shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the Chief Justice,
(b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-three years,
(c) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council, on the ground of his or her incompetence, bad conduct, failure to perform his or her duties honestly, performance of business with mala fide intention or serious violation by him or her of the code of conduct required to be observed by him or her,
(d) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council on the ground that he or she is unable to discharge his or her duties because of physical or mental illness,
(e) if he or she is punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude,
(f) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of Chief Judge or Judge of a High Court shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the Chief Justice,
(b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-three years,
(c) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council, on the ground of his or her incompetence, bad conduct, failure to perform his or her duties honestly, performance of business with mala fide intention or serious violation by him or her of the code of conduct required to be observed by him or her,
(d) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council on the ground that he or she is unable to discharge his or her duties because of physical or mental illness,
(e) if he or she is punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude,
(f) if he or she dies.

title: Vacation of office of Chief Judge or Judge
text:
Part 11 Article 142
Clause 2
Vacation of office of Chief Judge or Judge
A Judge who is facing a charge shall be provided with a reasonable opportunity to defend himself or herself prior to removing him or her from the office under sub-clause (c) of clause (1). The Judge against whom the proceedings have been so instituted shall not be allowed to perform the duties of his or her office until the proceedings are completed.
raw_text: A Judge who is facing a charge shall be provided with a reasonable opportunity to defend himself or herself prior to removing him or her from the office under sub-clause (c) of clause (1). The Judge against whom the proceedings have been so instituted shall not be allowed to perform the duties of his or her office until the proceedings are completed.

title: Vacation of office of Chief Judge or Judge
text:
Part 11 Article 142
Clause 3
Vacation of office of Chief Judge or Judge
Nothing shall bar the institution of action in accordance with the Federal law against the Chief Judge or Judge who has been removed from office for an offence which he or she has committed while in office.
raw_text: Nothing shall bar the institution of action in accordance with the Federal law against the Chief Judge or Judge who has been removed from office for an offence which he or she has committed while in office.

title: Chief Judge and Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
text:
Part 11 Article 143
Clause 1
Chief Judge and Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
No Chief Judge or Judge of a High Court shall be engaged in or deputed to any office other than that of Judge.
Provided that the Government of Nepal may, in consultation with the Judicial Council, depute a Judge of the High Court to work concerning judicial
inquiry, or to legal or judicial investigation or research or to any business of national concern for any specific period.
raw_text:
No Chief Judge or Judge of a High Court shall be engaged in or deputed to any office other than that of Judge.
Provided that the Government of Nepal may, in consultation with the Judicial Council, depute a Judge of the High Court to work concerning judicial
inquiry, or to legal or judicial investigation or research or to any business of national concern for any specific period.

title: Chief Judge and Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
text:
Part 11 Article 143
Clause 2
Chief Judge and Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
The Chief Justice may, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, transfer a Judge of a High Court to another High Court.
raw_text: The Chief Justice may, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, transfer a Judge of a High Court to another High Court.

title: Jurisdiction of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 144
Clause 1
Jurisdiction of High Court
The High Court shall have the power to issue necessary and appropriate orders, for the enforcement of the fundamental rights conferred by this Constitution or for the enforcement of any other legal right for which no other remedy has been provided or for which the remedy even though provided appears to be inadequate or ineffective or for the settlement of any legal question involved in any dispute of public interest or concern,
raw_text: The High Court shall have the power to issue necessary and appropriate orders, for the enforcement of the fundamental rights conferred by this Constitution or for the enforcement of any other legal right for which no other remedy has been provided or for which the remedy even though provided appears to be inadequate or ineffective or for the settlement of any legal question involved in any dispute of public interest or concern,

title: Jurisdiction of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 144
Clause 2
Jurisdiction of High Court
For the purposes of clause (1), the High Court may issue appropriate orders and writs including the writs of habeas corpus, mandamus, certiorari, prohibition and quo warranto.
Provided that except on the ground of absence of jurisdiction, the High Court shall not under this clause interfere with any internal proceedings of the Federal Parliament or State Assembly, and with any proceedings instituted by the Federal Parliament or State Assembly concerning violation of its privileges and penalties imposed therefor.
raw_text:
For the purposes of clause (1), the High Court may issue appropriate orders and writs including the writs of habeas corpus, mandamus, certiorari, prohibition and quo warranto.
Provided that except on the ground of absence of jurisdiction, the High Court shall not under this clause interfere with any internal proceedings of the Federal Parliament or State Assembly, and with any proceedings instituted by the Federal Parliament or State Assembly concerning violation of its privileges and penalties imposed therefor.

title: Jurisdiction of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 144
Clause 3
Jurisdiction of High Court
The High Court shall, in accordance with the Federal law, have the power to originally try and settle cases, hear appeals and test judgments referred for confirmation.
raw_text: The High Court shall, in accordance with the Federal law, have the power to originally try and settle cases, hear appeals and test judgments referred for confirmation.

title: Jurisdiction of High Court
text:
Part 11 Article 144
Clause 4
Jurisdiction of High Court
Other powers and procedures of the High Court shall be as provided by the Federal law.
raw_text: Other powers and procedures of the High Court shall be as provided by the Federal law.

title: Power to transfer cases
text:
Part 11 Article 145
Clause 1
Power to transfer cases
If a High Court is of the opinion that a case which is sub judice in a subordinate court within its jurisdiction involves a question relating to a State law and it is essential to settle that question in order to
decide the case, the High Court may procure the case and dispose the case wholly or decide only such question and remand the case to the court of first instance.
raw_text:
If a High Court is of the opinion that a case which is sub judice in a subordinate court within its jurisdiction involves a question relating to a State law and it is essential to settle that question in order to
decide the case, the High Court may procure the case and dispose the case wholly or decide only such question and remand the case to the court of first instance.

title: Power to transfer cases
text:
Part 11 Article 145
Clause 2
Power to transfer cases
If it is satisfied with the reason that there exists a situation where judicial impartiality can be questioned if a case filed in a District Court is tried by that Court, the High Court may, for reasons and grounds to be recorded, order such case to be transferred from such District Court to another District Court under its jurisdiction and tried by the latter District Court, as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: If it is satisfied with the reason that there exists a situation where judicial impartiality can be questioned if a case filed in a District Court is tried by that Court, the High Court may, for reasons and grounds to be recorded, order such case to be transferred from such District Court to another District Court under its jurisdiction and tried by the latter District Court, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: To be engaged in practice of law
text:
Part 11 Article 146
To be engaged in practice of law
A person who has retired from the office of a Judge of a High Court may be engaged in the practice of law before the Supreme Court and a High Court, other than the High Court where he or she has served as a Judge and a subordinate court.
raw_text: A person who has retired from the office of a Judge of a High Court may be engaged in the practice of law before the Supreme Court and a High Court, other than the High Court where he or she has served as a Judge and a subordinate court.

title: Responsibility of Chief Judge
text:
Part 11 Article 147
Responsibility of Chief Judge
The Chief Judge shall have the ultimate responsibility to make effective the administration of justice by the High Court and the Courts or other judicial bodies which are subordinate to it. For that purpose, the Chief Judge may, subject to this Constitution and the Federal law, give necessary direction to the subordinate Courts and judicial bodies.
raw_text: The Chief Judge shall have the ultimate responsibility to make effective the administration of justice by the High Court and the Courts or other judicial bodies which are subordinate to it. For that purpose, the Chief Judge may, subject to this Constitution and the Federal law, give necessary direction to the subordinate Courts and judicial bodies.

title: District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 148
Clause 1
District Courts
There shall be a District Court in each District.
raw_text: There shall be a District Court in each District.

title: District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 148
Clause 2
District Courts
The Local level judicial bodies established in accordance with the State law shall be subordinate to the District Court. The District Court may inspect as well as supervise and give necessary direction to its subordinate judicial bodies.
raw_text: The Local level judicial bodies established in accordance with the State law shall be subordinate to the District Court. The District Court may inspect as well as supervise and give necessary direction to its subordinate judicial bodies.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 1
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
The Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, appoint Judges of the District Courts.
raw_text: The Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, appoint Judges of the District Courts.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 2
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
The vacant posts of Judges of the District Court shall be filled as follows:
(a) twenty percent of the vacant posts, on the basis of evaluation of seniority, qualification and competency, from amongst the officers who have obtained bachelor's degree in law and served for at least three years in the post of Gazetted Second Class of the Judicial Service,
(b) forty percent of the vacant posts, on the basis of open competitive examination, from amongst the officers who have obtained bachelor's degree in law and served for at least three years in the post of Gazetted Second Class of the Judicial Service,
(c) the remaining forty percent of the vacant posts, on the basis of open competitive examination, from amongst the citizens of Nepal who, having obtained bachelor's degree in law, have constantly practiced law for at least eight years as an advocate or who, having obtained bachelor's degree in law, have served in a Gazetted post of the Judicial Service for at least eight years or have constantly been engaged in the teaching or research of law or served in any other field of law or justice for at least eight years.
raw_text:
The vacant posts of Judges of the District Court shall be filled as follows:
(a) twenty percent of the vacant posts, on the basis of evaluation of seniority, qualification and competency, from amongst the officers who have obtained bachelor's degree in law and served for at least three years in the post of Gazetted Second Class of the Judicial Service,
(b) forty percent of the vacant posts, on the basis of open competitive examination, from amongst the officers who have obtained bachelor's degree in law and served for at least three years in the post of Gazetted Second Class of the Judicial Service,
(c) the remaining forty percent of the vacant posts, on the basis of open competitive examination, from amongst the citizens of Nepal who, having obtained bachelor's degree in law, have constantly practiced law for at least eight years as an advocate or who, having obtained bachelor's degree in law, have served in a Gazetted post of the Judicial Service for at least eight years or have constantly been engaged in the teaching or research of law or served in any other field of law or justice for at least eight years.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 3
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
The Judicial Service Commission shall, in accordance with the Federal law, hold written and oral competitive examinations for the persons who have possessed the qualification under sub-clauses (b) and (c) of clause (2), and make recommendation, in order of merit, to the Judicial Council for appointment as District Judges.
raw_text: The Judicial Service Commission shall, in accordance with the Federal law, hold written and oral competitive examinations for the persons who have possessed the qualification under sub-clauses (b) and (c) of clause (2), and make recommendation, in order of merit, to the Judicial Council for appointment as District Judges.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 4
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Judges of the District Court shall be provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Judges of the District Court shall be provided for in the Federal law.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 5
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
The remuneration and other conditions of service of a Judge of the District Court shall not be altered to his or her disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.
raw_text:
The remuneration and other conditions of service of a Judge of the District Court shall not be altered to his or her disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 6
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
The office of a Judge of the District Court shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the Chief Justice,
(b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-three years,
(c) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council, on the ground of his or her incompetence, misconduct, failure to perform his or her duties honestly, performance of business with mala fide intention or serious violation by him or her of the code of conduct required to be observed by him or her,
(d) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council on the ground that he or she is unable to continue service and discharge his or her duties because of physical or mental illness,
(e) if he or she is punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude,
(f) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of a Judge of the District Court shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the Chief Justice,
(b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-three years,
(c) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council, on the ground of his or her incompetence, misconduct, failure to perform his or her duties honestly, performance of business with mala fide intention or serious violation by him or her of the code of conduct required to be observed by him or her,
(d) if he or she is removed from office by the Chief Justice on recommendation of the Judicial Council on the ground that he or she is unable to continue service and discharge his or her duties because of physical or mental illness,
(e) if he or she is punished by a court for a criminal offence involving moral turpitude,
(f) if he or she dies.

title: Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
text:
Part 11 Article 149
Clause 7
Appointment, qualification, remuneration and other conditions of service of Judges of District Courts
A District Judge who is accused shall be given a reasonable opportunity to defend himself or herself before removing him or her from office under sub-clause (c) of clause (6). The District Judge against whom the proceedings are so initiated shall not perform the duties of his or her office until the proceedings are completed.
raw_text: A District Judge who is accused shall be given a reasonable opportunity to defend himself or herself before removing him or her from office under sub-clause (c) of clause (6). The District Judge against whom the proceedings are so initiated shall not perform the duties of his or her office until the proceedings are completed.

title: District Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
text:
Part 11 Article 150
Clause 1
District Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
No District Judge shall be engaged in or deputed to any office other than that of Judge.
Provided that the Government of Nepal may, in consultation with the Judicial Council, depute a District Judge to work concerning judicial inquiry, or to legal or judicial investigation or research and to any election related work, for a specified period.
raw_text:
No District Judge shall be engaged in or deputed to any office other than that of Judge.
Provided that the Government of Nepal may, in consultation with the Judicial Council, depute a District Judge to work concerning judicial inquiry, or to legal or judicial investigation or research and to any election related work, for a specified period.

title: District Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
text:
Part 11 Article 150
Clause 2
District Judges not to be engaged in any other office and provisions relating to transfer
The Chief Justice may, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, transfer a District Judge from one District Court to another District Court.
raw_text: The Chief Justice may, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, transfer a District Judge from one District Court to another District Court.

title: Jurisdiction of District Court
text:
Part 11 Article 151
Clause 1
Jurisdiction of District Court
Except as otherwise provided by the Federal law, a District Court shall have the power to originally try and settle all cases under its jurisdiction, to try petitions under law, including petitions of habeas corpus and prohibition, hear appeals under law from decisions made by quasi- judicial bodies, hear appeals from decisions made by Local level judicial bodies formed under the State law, institute contempt proceedings and punish for contempt under the Federal law if anyone makes obstruction in the dispensation of justice by, or disregards any order or judgment by, it or any of its subordinate courts.
raw_text: Except as otherwise provided by the Federal law, a District Court shall have the power to originally try and settle all cases under its jurisdiction, to try petitions under law, including petitions of habeas corpus and prohibition, hear appeals under law from decisions made by quasi- judicial bodies, hear appeals from decisions made by Local level judicial bodies formed under the State law, institute contempt proceedings and punish for contempt under the Federal law if anyone makes obstruction in the dispensation of justice by, or disregards any order or judgment by, it or any of its subordinate courts.

title: Jurisdiction of District Court
text:
Part 11 Article 151
Clause 2
Jurisdiction of District Court
Other provisions relating to the jurisdiction and procedures of the District Courts shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other provisions relating to the jurisdiction and procedures of the District Courts shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Specialized courts
text:
Part 11 Article 152
Clause 1
Specialized courts
Other specialized courts, judicial bodies or tribunals may be formed to try and settle specific types and nature of cases other than those mentioned in Article 127, as provided for in the Federal law.
Provided that no specialized court, judicial body or tribunal shall be formed for any specific case.
raw_text:
Other specialized courts, judicial bodies or tribunals may be formed to try and settle specific types and nature of cases other than those mentioned in Article 127, as provided for in the Federal law.
Provided that no specialized court, judicial body or tribunal shall be formed for any specific case.

title: Specialized courts
text:
Part 11 Article 152
Clause 2
Specialized courts
No criminal offence involving imprisonment for a term of more than one year shall fall under the jurisdiction of a body other than a court, specialized court, military court or judicial body.
raw_text: No criminal offence involving imprisonment for a term of more than one year shall fall under the jurisdiction of a body other than a court, specialized court, military court or judicial body.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 1
Judicial Council
There shall be a Judicial Council to make recommendation or give advice, in accordance with this Constitution, on the appointment of, transfer of, disciplinary action against, and dismissal of, Judges, and other matters relating to the administration of justice, which shall consist of the following as its chairperson and members:
(a) The Chief Justice - Chairperson
(b) The Federal Minister for Law and Justice - Member
(c) The senior-most Judge of the Supreme Court - Member
(d) One jurist nominated by the President on recommendation of the Prime Minister - Member
(e) A senior advocate or advocate who has gained at least twenty years of experience, to be appointed by the President on recommendation of the Nepal Bar Association - Member
raw_text:
There shall be a Judicial Council to make recommendation or give advice, in accordance with this Constitution, on the appointment of, transfer of, disciplinary action against, and dismissal of, Judges, and other matters relating to the administration of justice, which shall consist of the following as its chairperson and members:
(a) The Chief Justice - Chairperson
(b) The Federal Minister for Law and Justice - Member
(c) The senior-most Judge of the Supreme Court - Member
(d) One jurist nominated by the President on recommendation of the Prime Minister - Member
(e) A senior advocate or advocate who has gained at least twenty years of experience, to be appointed by the President on recommendation of the Nepal Bar Association - Member

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 2
Judicial Council
The term of office of the members under sub-clauses (d) and (e) of clause (1) shall be four years, and their remuneration and facilities shall be equivalent to those of a Judge of the Supreme Court.
raw_text: The term of office of the members under sub-clauses (d) and (e) of clause (1) shall be four years, and their remuneration and facilities shall be equivalent to those of a Judge of the Supreme Court.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 3
Judicial Council
The members under sub-clauses (d) and (e) of clause (1) shall be removed from office in the same manner and on the same grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court.
raw_text: The members under sub-clauses (d) and (e) of clause (1) shall be removed from office in the same manner and on the same grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 4
Judicial Council
The chairperson and a member of the Judicial Council may study the case associated with a complaint filed in respect of any Judge, and make a report thereof to the Judicial Council.
raw_text: The chairperson and a member of the Judicial Council may study the case associated with a complaint filed in respect of any Judge, and make a report thereof to the Judicial Council.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 5
Judicial Council
If a preliminary inquiry of a complaint filed in respect of any Judge reveals a need to have a detailed inquiry by an expert, the Judicial Council may form an inquiry committee.
raw_text: If a preliminary inquiry of a complaint filed in respect of any Judge reveals a need to have a detailed inquiry by an expert, the Judicial Council may form an inquiry committee.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 6
Judicial Council
If a Judge, other than a Judge who can be removed from office by way of impeachment in accordance with this Constitution, commits an abuse of authority by way of corruption, the Judicial Council may investigate into the matter and institute a case in accordance with law.
raw_text: If a Judge, other than a Judge who can be removed from office by way of impeachment in accordance with this Constitution, commits an abuse of authority by way of corruption, the Judicial Council may investigate into the matter and institute a case in accordance with law.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 7
Judicial Council
The Judicial Council must prepare updated records of the persons who are qualified for appointment to offices of the Chief Justice, Judges of the Supreme Court, Chief Judges and Judges of the High Courts.
raw_text: The Judicial Council must prepare updated records of the persons who are qualified for appointment to offices of the Chief Justice, Judges of the Supreme Court, Chief Judges and Judges of the High Courts.

title: Judicial Council
text:
Part 11 Article 153
Clause 8
Judicial Council
Other functions, duties and powers of the Judicial Council shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other functions, duties and powers of the Judicial Council shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Judicial Service Commission
text:
Part 11 Article 154
Clause 1
Judicial Service Commission
In appointing, transferring or promoting gazetted officers of the Federal Judicial Service or taking departmental action concerning such officers in accordance with law, the Government of Nepal shall act on recommendation of the Judicial Service Commission.
Provided that in making new permanent appointments to gazetted posts of the Federal Judicial Service from persons who are not already in the Federal
Government Service or making promotions from non-gazetted posts of the Federal Judicial Service to gazetted ones of that Service, the Government of Nepal shall act on the recommendation of the Public Service Commission. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, the Public Service Commission shall hold open and internal competitive examinations to be held for appointments to Gazetted posts of the Federal Judicial Service.
raw_text:
In appointing, transferring or promoting gazetted officers of the Federal Judicial Service or taking departmental action concerning such officers in accordance with law, the Government of Nepal shall act on recommendation of the Judicial Service Commission.
Provided that in making new permanent appointments to gazetted posts of the Federal Judicial Service from persons who are not already in the Federal
Government Service or making promotions from non-gazetted posts of the Federal Judicial Service to gazetted ones of that Service, the Government of Nepal shall act on the recommendation of the Public Service Commission. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, the Public Service Commission shall hold open and internal competitive examinations to be held for appointments to Gazetted posts of the Federal Judicial Service.

title: Judicial Service Commission
text:
Part 11 Article 154
Clause 2
Judicial Service Commission
The Judicial Service Commission shall consist of the following as its chairperson and members:
(a) The Chief Justice - Chairperson
(b) The Federal Minister for Law and Justice - Member
(c) The seniormost Judge of the Supreme Court - Member
(d) The Chairperson of the Public Service Commission - Member
(e) The Attorney General - Member
raw_text:
The Judicial Service Commission shall consist of the following as its chairperson and members:
(a) The Chief Justice - Chairperson
(b) The Federal Minister for Law and Justice - Member
(c) The seniormost Judge of the Supreme Court - Member
(d) The Chairperson of the Public Service Commission - Member
(e) The Attorney General - Member

title: Judicial Service Commission
text:
Part 11 Article 154
Clause 3
Judicial Service Commission
Other functions, duties, powers and procedures of the Judicial Service Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other functions, duties, powers and procedures of the Judicial Service Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to conditions of service and facilities
text:
Part 11 Article 155
Provisions relating to conditions of service and facilities
Provisions relating to the remuneration, facilities and conditions of service of the employees of the Federal Judicial Service shall be as provided for in the Federal Act.
raw_text: Provisions relating to the remuneration, facilities and conditions of service of the employees of the Federal Judicial Service shall be as provided for in the Federal Act.

title: Provisions relating to State Judicial Service Commission
text:
Part 11 Article 156
Provisions relating to State Judicial Service Commission
Provisions relating to the formation of a State Judicial Service Commission and the remuneration,
facilities and conditions of service of the employees of a State Judicial Service shall be as provided for in the Federal law
raw_text:
Provisions relating to the formation of a State Judicial Service Commission and the remuneration,
facilities and conditions of service of the employees of a State Judicial Service shall be as provided for in the Federal law

title: Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 157
Clause 1
Attorney General
There shall be an Attorney General of Nepal.
raw_text: There shall be an Attorney General of Nepal.

title: Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 157
Clause 2
Attorney General
The President shall, on recommendation of the Prime Minister, appoint the Attorney General.  The Attorney General shall hold office during the pleasure of the Prime Minister.
raw_text: The President shall, on recommendation of the Prime Minister, appoint the Attorney General.  The Attorney General shall hold office during the pleasure of the Prime Minister.

title: Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 157
Clause 3
Attorney General
A person who is qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be eligible to be appointed as the Attorney General.
raw_text: A person who is qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court shall be eligible to be appointed as the Attorney General.

title: Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 157
Clause 4
Attorney General
The office of the Attorney General shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President through the Prime Minister,
(b) if he or she is removed from office by the President on recommendation of the Prime Minister,
(c) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Attorney General shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President through the Prime Minister,
(b) if he or she is removed from office by the President on recommendation of the Prime Minister,
(c) if he or she dies.

title: Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 157
Clause 5
Attorney General
The remuneration and other facilities of the Attorney General shall be similar to those of a Judge of the Supreme Court. Other conditions of service of the Attorney General shall be in accordance with law.
raw_text: The remuneration and other facilities of the Attorney General shall be similar to those of a Judge of the Supreme Court. Other conditions of service of the Attorney General shall be in accordance with law.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 158
Clause 1
Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
The Attorney General shall be the chief legal advisor to the Government of Nepal. It shall be the duty of the Attorney General to give opinions and advices on constitutional and legal matters to the Government of Nepal and such other authorities as the Government of Nepal may specify.
raw_text: The Attorney General shall be the chief legal advisor to the Government of Nepal. It shall be the duty of the Attorney General to give opinions and advices on constitutional and legal matters to the Government of Nepal and such other authorities as the Government of Nepal may specify.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 158
Clause 2
Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
The Attorney General or government attorneys subordinate to him or her shall represent the Government of Nepal in lawsuits wherein the rights, interests or concerns of the Government of Nepal are involved. Except
as provided otherwise in this Constitution, the Attorney General shall have the right to make a final decision as to whether to institute any case on behalf of the Government of Nepal in any court, judicial body or authority.
raw_text:
The Attorney General or government attorneys subordinate to him or her shall represent the Government of Nepal in lawsuits wherein the rights, interests or concerns of the Government of Nepal are involved. Except
as provided otherwise in this Constitution, the Attorney General shall have the right to make a final decision as to whether to institute any case on behalf of the Government of Nepal in any court, judicial body or authority.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 158
Clause 3
Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
Opinion of the Attorney General must be obtained for withdrawing a case filed on behalf of the Government of Nepal.
raw_text: Opinion of the Attorney General must be obtained for withdrawing a case filed on behalf of the Government of Nepal.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 158
Clause 4
Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
The Attorney General may, upon invitation by the Federal Parliament or any of its committees, to appear and express his or her opinion on any legal question in such a meeting.
raw_text: The Attorney General may, upon invitation by the Federal Parliament or any of its committees, to appear and express his or her opinion on any legal question in such a meeting.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 158
Clause 5
Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
In the course of discharging the duties of his or her office, the Attorney General shall have the right to appear in any Court, office and authority of Nepal.
raw_text: In the course of discharging the duties of his or her office, the Attorney General shall have the right to appear in any Court, office and authority of Nepal.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
text:
Part 12 Article 158
Clause 6
Functions, duties and powers of Attorney General
The Attorney General shall, in the course of performing his or her duties, have the power to carry out the following acts, in addition to that set forth in clause (2):
(a) to defend, on behalf of the Government of Nepal, any lawsuit in which the Government of Nepal is a plaintiff or a defendant,
