If any Bill is sent back with his or her message by the Chief of State under clause (3), it shall be reconsidered by the State Assembly and if the Bill so reconsidered is again passed as it was or with amendments, and is again presented to him or her, the Chief of State shall give assent to that Bill within fifteen days of such presentation.
raw_text: If any Bill is sent back with his or her message by the Chief of State under clause (3), it shall be reconsidered by the State Assembly and if the Bill so reconsidered is again passed as it was or with amendments, and is again presented to him or her, the Chief of State shall give assent to that Bill within fifteen days of such presentation.

title: Assent on Bills
text:
Part 15 Article 201
Clause 5
Assent on Bills
A Bill shall become an Act after the Chief of State gives assent to it.
raw_text: A Bill shall become an Act after the Chief of State gives assent to it.

title: Ordinance
text:
Part 15 Article 202
Clause 1
Ordinance
If, at any time, except when the State Assembly is in session, a circumstance exists which renders it necessary to take immediate action, the Chief of State may, on the recommendation of the State Council of Ministers, promulgate an Ordinance.
raw_text: If, at any time, except when the State Assembly is in session, a circumstance exists which renders it necessary to take immediate action, the Chief of State may, on the recommendation of the State Council of Ministers, promulgate an Ordinance.

title: Ordinance
text:
Part 15 Article 202
Clause 2
Ordinance
An Ordinance promulgated under clause (1) shall have the same force and effect as an Act.
Provided that every such Ordinance:
(a) shall be tabled at the session of the State Assembly held after the promulgation, and if not passed by the State Assembly, it shall ipso facto cease to be effective,
(b) may be repealed at any time by the Chief of State, and
(c) shall, unless rendered ineffective or repealed under sub-clause (a) or (b), ipso facto cease to be effective at the expiration of sixty days after the day on which a meeting of the State Assembly is held.
raw_text:
An Ordinance promulgated under clause (1) shall have the same force and effect as an Act.
Provided that every such Ordinance:
(a) shall be tabled at the session of the State Assembly held after the promulgation, and if not passed by the State Assembly, it shall ipso facto cease to be effective,
(b) may be repealed at any time by the Chief of State, and
(c) shall, unless rendered ineffective or repealed under sub-clause (a) or (b), ipso facto cease to be effective at the expiration of sixty days after the day on which a meeting of the State Assembly is held.

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text:
Part 16 Article 203
Clause 1
No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
No tax shall be levied and collected in a State except in accordance with law.
raw_text: No tax shall be levied and collected in a State except in accordance with law.

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text:
Part 16 Article 203
Clause 2
No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
No loan shall be raised and guarantee given by the State Government except as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: No loan shall be raised and guarantee given by the State Government except as provided for in the Federal law.

title: State Consolidated Fund
text:
Part 16 Article 204
State Consolidated Fund
Except for the revenues of trusts (Guthi), all revenues received by the State Government, all loans raised on the security of revenues, all moneys received in repayment of any loans made under the authority of any State Act and any mounts of grants or loans received from the Government of Nepal shall, except as otherwise provided for in the State Act, be credited to a State Government Fund to be known as the State Consolidated Fund.
raw_text: Except for the revenues of trusts (Guthi), all revenues received by the State Government, all loans raised on the security of revenues, all moneys received in repayment of any loans made under the authority of any State Act and any mounts of grants or loans received from the Government of Nepal shall, except as otherwise provided for in the State Act, be credited to a State Government Fund to be known as the State Consolidated Fund.

title: Expenditures from State Consolidated Fund or State Government Fund
text:
Part 16 Article 205
Expenditures from State Consolidated Fund or State Government Fund
No expenditure shall be incurred out of the State Consolidated Fund or any other State Government Fund except the following: (a) moneys charged on the State Consolidated Fund, (b) moneys required to meet the expenditure under an Appropriation Act, (c) advance moneys authorized by an Act required to meet expenditures, when an Appropriation Bill is under consideration, or (d) expenditures to be incurred in extraordinary circumstances under a Vote of Credit Act which contains only a description of expenditures.
Provided that matters relating to the State Contingency Fund shall be in accordance with Article 212.
raw_text:
No expenditure shall be incurred out of the State Consolidated Fund or any other State Government Fund except the following: (a) moneys charged on the State Consolidated Fund, (b) moneys required to meet the expenditure under an Appropriation Act, (c) advance moneys authorized by an Act required to meet expenditures, when an Appropriation Bill is under consideration, or (d) expenditures to be incurred in extraordinary circumstances under a Vote of Credit Act which contains only a description of expenditures.
Provided that matters relating to the State Contingency Fund shall be in accordance with Article 212.

title: Expenditures chargeable on State Consolidated Fund
text:
Part 16 Article 206
Expenditures chargeable on State Consolidated Fund
The expenditures relating to the following matters shall be charged on the State Consolidated Fund, and approval of the State Assembly shall not be required for such expenditures: (a) the amount required as remuneration and facilities payable to the State Speaker and the State Deputy Speaker, (b) the amount required as remuneration and facilities payable to the Chairperson and members of the State Public Service Commission, (c) all charges relating to debts for which the State Government is liable, (d) any sum required to satisfy any judgment or decree made by a court against the State Government, and (e) any other sum specified by a State law to be chargeable on the State Consolidated Fund.
raw_text: The expenditures relating to the following matters shall be charged on the State Consolidated Fund, and approval of the State Assembly shall not be required for such expenditures: (a) the amount required as remuneration and facilities payable to the State Speaker and the State Deputy Speaker, (b) the amount required as remuneration and facilities payable to the Chairperson and members of the State Public Service Commission, (c) all charges relating to debts for which the State Government is liable, (d) any sum required to satisfy any judgment or decree made by a court against the State Government, and (e) any other sum specified by a State law to be chargeable on the State Consolidated Fund.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text:
Part 16 Article 207
Clause 1
Estimates of revenues and expenditures
The State Minister for Finance shall, in respect of every financial year, lay before the State Assembly an annual estimate setting out, inter alia, the following matters:
(a) an estimate of revenues,
(b) the moneys required to meet the charges on the State Consolidated Fund, and
(c) the moneys required to meet the expenditure to be provided for by a State Appropriation Act.
raw_text:
The State Minister for Finance shall, in respect of every financial year, lay before the State Assembly an annual estimate setting out, inter alia, the following matters:
(a) an estimate of revenues,
(b) the moneys required to meet the charges on the State Consolidated Fund, and
(c) the moneys required to meet the expenditure to be provided for by a State Appropriation Act.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text:
Part 16 Article 207
Clause 2
Estimates of revenues and expenditures
The annual estimate to be laid pursuant to clause (1) shall also be accompanied by a statement of the expenses allocated to every Ministry in the
previous financial year and particulars of whether the objectives of the expenses have been achieved.
raw_text:
The annual estimate to be laid pursuant to clause (1) shall also be accompanied by a statement of the expenses allocated to every Ministry in the
previous financial year and particulars of whether the objectives of the expenses have been achieved.

title: State Appropriation Act
text:
Part 16 Article 208
State Appropriation Act
The moneys required to meet the expenditure to be provided for by any State Appropriation Act shall be specified under appropriate heads in an Appropriation Bill.
raw_text: The moneys required to meet the expenditure to be provided for by any State Appropriation Act shall be specified under appropriate heads in an Appropriation Bill.

title: Supplementary estimates
text:
Part 16 Article 209
Clause 1
Supplementary estimates
The State Minister for Finance may lay before the State Assembly a supplementary estimate if it is found in any financial year,-
(a) that the sum authorized to be spent for a particular service by the State Appropriation Act for the current financial year is insufficient, or that a need has arisen for expenditures upon some new service not provided for in the State Appropriation Act for that year, or
(b) that the expenditures made during that financial year are in excess of the amount authorized by the State Appropriation Act.
raw_text:
The State Minister for Finance may lay before the State Assembly a supplementary estimate if it is found in any financial year,-
(a) that the sum authorized to be spent for a particular service by the State Appropriation Act for the current financial year is insufficient, or that a need has arisen for expenditures upon some new service not provided for in the State Appropriation Act for that year, or
(b) that the expenditures made during that financial year are in excess of the amount authorized by the State Appropriation Act.

title: Supplementary estimates
text:
Part 16 Article 209
Clause 2
Supplementary estimates
The sums included in the supplementary estimates shall be specified under the heads in a Supplementary Appropriation Bill.
raw_text: The sums included in the supplementary estimates shall be specified under the heads in a Supplementary Appropriation Bill.

title: Votes on Account
text:
Part 16 Article 210
Clause 1
Votes on Account
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, a portion of the expenditure estimated for the financial year may, when a State Appropriation Bill is under consideration, be incurred in advance under a State Act.
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, a portion of the expenditure estimated for the financial year may, when a State Appropriation Bill is under consideration, be incurred in advance under a State Act.

title: Votes on Account
text:
Part 16 Article 210
Clause 2
Votes on Account
A Vote on Account Bill shall not be introduced until the estimates of revenues and expenditures have been laid in accordance with Article 207, and the sums involved in the Vote on Account shall not exceed one-third of the estimate of expenditures for the financial year.
raw_text: A Vote on Account Bill shall not be introduced until the estimates of revenues and expenditures have been laid in accordance with Article 207, and the sums involved in the Vote on Account shall not exceed one-third of the estimate of expenditures for the financial year.

title: Votes on Account
text:
Part 16 Article 210
Clause 3
Votes on Account
The expenditures incurred in accordance with the State Vote on Account Act shall be included in the State Appropriation Bill.
raw_text: The expenditures incurred in accordance with the State Vote on Account Act shall be included in the State Appropriation Bill.

title: Votes of Credit
text:
Part 16 Article 211
Votes of Credit
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, if owing to an emergency due to natural causes or other reasons, it appears to be impractical or inexpedient in view of the security or interest of the State to specify the details required under clause (1) of Article 207, the State Minister for Finance may lay before the State Assembly a Vote of Credit Bill giving only a statement of expenditures.
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, if owing to an emergency due to natural causes or other reasons, it appears to be impractical or inexpedient in view of the security or interest of the State to specify the details required under clause (1) of Article 207, the State Minister for Finance may lay before the State Assembly a Vote of Credit Bill giving only a statement of expenditures.

title: State Contingency Fund
text:
Part 16 Article 212
Clause 1
State Contingency Fund
A State Act may create a Fund to be known as the State Contingency Fund into which shall be paid from time to time such moneys as may be determined by the State Act.
raw_text: A State Act may create a Fund to be known as the State Contingency Fund into which shall be paid from time to time such moneys as may be determined by the State Act.

title: State Contingency Fund
text:
Part 16 Article 212
Clause 2
State Contingency Fund
The Fund under clause (1) shall be under the control of the State Government. Any unforeseen expenditure may be met out of such Fund by the State Government.
raw_text: The Fund under clause (1) shall be under the control of the State Government. Any unforeseen expenditure may be met out of such Fund by the State Government.

title: State Contingency Fund
text:
Part 16 Article 212
Clause 3
State Contingency Fund
The amount of the expenditure under clause (2) shall be reimbursed as soon as possible by the State Act.
raw_text: The amount of the expenditure under clause (2) shall be reimbursed as soon as possible by the State Act.

title: Act relating to financial procedures
text:
Part 16 Article 213
Act relating to financial procedures
Matters relating to the transfer of moneys appropriated by the State Act from one head to another and other financial procedures shall be as provided for in the State Act
raw_text: Matters relating to the transfer of moneys appropriated by the State Act from one head to another and other financial procedures shall be as provided for in the State Act

title: Executive power of Local Level
text:
Part 17 Article 214
Clause 1
Executive power of Local Level
The executive power of the Local Level shall, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal law, be vested in the Village Executive or the Municipal Executive.
raw_text: The executive power of the Local Level shall, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal law, be vested in the Village Executive or the Municipal Executive.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text:
Part 17 Article 214
Clause 2
Executive power of Local Level
The local executive power shall be as mentioned in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.
raw_text: The local executive power shall be as mentioned in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text:
Part 17 Article 214
Clause 3
Executive power of Local Level
The responsibility for issuing general directives, controlling and regulating the governance of the Village Body and the Municipality shall, subject to this Constitution and other laws, lie in the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive.
raw_text: The responsibility for issuing general directives, controlling and regulating the governance of the Village Body and the Municipality shall, subject to this Constitution and other laws, lie in the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text:
Part 17 Article 214
Clause 4
Executive power of Local Level
The executive functions of the Village Body and the Municipality shall be performed in the name of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive.
raw_text: The executive functions of the Village Body and the Municipality shall be performed in the name of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text:
Part 17 Article 214
Clause 5
Executive power of Local Level
Any decision or order to be issued in the name of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive pursuant to clause (4) and other instruments of authorization pertaining thereto shall be authenticated as provided for in the Local law.
raw_text: Any decision or order to be issued in the name of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive pursuant to clause (4) and other instruments of authorization pertaining thereto shall be authenticated as provided for in the Local law.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
There shall be a Chairperson of Village Executive in each Village Body. The Village Executive shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.
raw_text: There shall be a Chairperson of Village Executive in each Village Body. The Village Executive shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
The Village Executive under clause (1) shall consist of one Vice- Chairperson, Ward Chairperson elected from each Ward and members elected pursuant to clause (4).
raw_text: The Village Executive under clause (1) shall consist of one Vice- Chairperson, Ward Chairperson elected from each Ward and members elected pursuant to clause (4).

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 3
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
The Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson shall be elected by the voters residing within the concerned Village Body area by secret ballots on the
basis of one person one vote, in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "Chairperson" and "Vice- Chairperson" mean the Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson of the Village Executive.
raw_text:
The Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson shall be elected by the voters residing within the concerned Village Body area by secret ballots on the
basis of one person one vote, in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "Chairperson" and "Vice- Chairperson" mean the Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson of the Village Executive.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 4
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
The members of the Village Executive shall also include four women members elected by the members of the Village Assembly from amongst themselves and two members elected by the Village Assembly from the Dalit or minority communities, in possession of the qualification under clause (5), no later than fifteen days after the final results of the election to the Village Assembly under Article 222.
raw_text: The members of the Village Executive shall also include four women members elected by the members of the Village Assembly from amongst themselves and two members elected by the Village Assembly from the Dalit or minority communities, in possession of the qualification under clause (5), no later than fifteen days after the final results of the election to the Village Assembly under Article 222.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 5
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to be elected to the office of the Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Ward Chairperson and member:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being a voter whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Village Body,
(d) not being disqualified by any law.
raw_text:
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to be elected to the office of the Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Ward Chairperson and member:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being a voter whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Village Body,
(d) not being disqualified by any law.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 6
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
The term of office of the Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Ward Chairperson and member shall be five years after the date of being elected.
raw_text: The term of office of the Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Ward Chairperson and member shall be five years after the date of being elected.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 7
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
A person who has been elected as the Chairperson for two terms shall not be eligible to be a candidate in an election to the Village Body.
raw_text: A person who has been elected as the Chairperson for two terms shall not be eligible to be a candidate in an election to the Village Body.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 8
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
The office of the Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Ward Chairperson or member shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the Chairperson tenders resignation in writing to the Vice-Chairperson, and if the Vice-Chairperson tenders resignation before the Chairperson,
(b) if his or her term of office expires,
(c) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson, Ward Chairperson or member shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the Chairperson tenders resignation in writing to the Vice-Chairperson, and if the Vice-Chairperson tenders resignation before the Chairperson,
(b) if his or her term of office expires,
(c) if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 215
Clause 9
Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
If the seat falls vacant under clause (8) while the term of office of the Chairperson or Vice-Chairperson still remains for more than one year, the vacancy shall be filled through by-election for the remainder of term.
raw_text: If the seat falls vacant under clause (8) while the term of office of the Chairperson or Vice-Chairperson still remains for more than one year, the vacancy shall be filled through by-election for the remainder of term.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
Each Municipality shall have a Mayor. The Municipal Executive shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.
raw_text: Each Municipality shall have a Mayor. The Municipal Executive shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
The Municipal Executive under clause (1) shall consist of one Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson elected from each Ward and members elected pursuant to clause (4).
raw_text: The Municipal Executive under clause (1) shall consist of one Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson elected from each Ward and members elected pursuant to clause (4).

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 3
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
The Mayor and the Deputy Mayor shall be elected by the voters residing within the concerned Municipal area by secret ballots on the basis of one person one vote, in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "Mayor" and "Deputy Mayor" mean the Mayor and the Deputy Mayor of the Municipal Executive.
raw_text:
The Mayor and the Deputy Mayor shall be elected by the voters residing within the concerned Municipal area by secret ballots on the basis of one person one vote, in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.
Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "Mayor" and "Deputy Mayor" mean the Mayor and the Deputy Mayor of the Municipal Executive.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 4
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
The members of the Municipal Executive shall also include five women members elected by the members of the Municipal Assembly from amongst themselves and three members elected by the Municipal Assembly from the Dalit or minority communities, in possession of the qualification under clause (5), no later than fifteen days after the final results of the election to the Municipal Assembly under Article 223.
raw_text: The members of the Municipal Executive shall also include five women members elected by the members of the Municipal Assembly from amongst themselves and three members elected by the Municipal Assembly from the Dalit or minority communities, in possession of the qualification under clause (5), no later than fifteen days after the final results of the election to the Municipal Assembly under Article 223.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 5
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to be elected to the office of the Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson and member:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being a voter whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality,
(d) not being disqualified by any law.
raw_text:
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to be elected to the office of the Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson and member:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being a voter whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality,
(d) not being disqualified by any law.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 6
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
The term of office of the Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson and member shall be five years after the date of being elected.
raw_text: The term of office of the Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson and member shall be five years after the date of being elected.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 7
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
A person who has been elected as the Mayor for two terms shall not be eligible to be a candidate in an election to the Municipality.
raw_text: A person who has been elected as the Mayor for two terms shall not be eligible to be a candidate in an election to the Municipality.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 8
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
The office of the Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson or Member shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the Mayor tenders resignation in writing to the Deputy Mayor, and if the Deputy Mayor tenders resignation in writing before the Mayor,
(b) if his or her term of office expires,
(c) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Ward Chairperson or Member shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the Mayor tenders resignation in writing to the Deputy Mayor, and if the Deputy Mayor tenders resignation in writing before the Mayor,
(b) if his or her term of office expires,
(c) if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 216
Clause 9
Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
If the seat falls vacant under clause (8) while the term of office of the Mayor or Deputy Mayor still remains for more than one year, the vacancy shall be filled through by-election for the remainder of term.
raw_text: If the seat falls vacant under clause (8) while the term of office of the Mayor or Deputy Mayor still remains for more than one year, the vacancy shall be filled through by-election for the remainder of term.

title: Judicial Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 217
Clause 1
Judicial Committee
There shall be a three-member judicial committee to be coordinated by its Vice-Chairperson in the case of a Village Body and by its Deputy Mayor in the case of a Municipality, in order to settle disputes under their respective jurisdictions in accordance with law.
raw_text: There shall be a three-member judicial committee to be coordinated by its Vice-Chairperson in the case of a Village Body and by its Deputy Mayor in the case of a Municipality, in order to settle disputes under their respective jurisdictions in accordance with law.

title: Judicial Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 217
Clause 2
Judicial Committee
The judicial committee under clause (1) shall consist of two members elected by the members of the Village Assembly or the Municipal Assembly from amongst themselves.
raw_text: The judicial committee under clause (1) shall consist of two members elected by the members of the Village Assembly or the Municipal Assembly from amongst themselves.

title: Conduct of business of Village Executive and Municipal Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 218
Conduct of business of Village Executive and Municipal Executive
Allocation and conduct of business of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive shall be carried out in accordance with the rules approved by the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive, respectively.
raw_text: Allocation and conduct of business of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive shall be carried out in accordance with the rules approved by the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive, respectively.

title: Other provisions relating to Local Level Executive
text:
Part 17 Article 219
Other provisions relating to Local Level Executive
Other provisions relating to the Local Level Executive, except those contained in this Part, shall be as provided for in the Federal law, subject to this Constitution.
raw_text: Other provisions relating to the Local Level Executive, except those contained in this Part, shall be as provided for in the Federal law, subject to this Constitution.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 1
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
There shall be a District Assembly to make coordination between the Village Bodies and Municipalities within the district.
raw_text: There shall be a District Assembly to make coordination between the Village Bodies and Municipalities within the district.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 2
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
The District Assembly shall consist of Chairpersons and Vice- Chairpersons of Village Executives, and Mayors and Deputy Mayors of Municipal Executives within the district. The first meeting of the District Assembly shall be held no later than thirty days of the date of final results of the election to the Village Assemblies and the Municipal Assemblies.
raw_text: The District Assembly shall consist of Chairpersons and Vice- Chairpersons of Village Executives, and Mayors and Deputy Mayors of Municipal Executives within the district. The first meeting of the District Assembly shall be held no later than thirty days of the date of final results of the election to the Village Assemblies and the Municipal Assemblies.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 3
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
The District Assembly shall elect the District Coordination Committee consisting of a maximum of nine Members including one Chief, one Deputy Chief, at least three women and at least one Dalit or minority. The District Coordination Committee shall discharge all functions required to be discharged by the District Assembly.
raw_text: The District Assembly shall elect the District Coordination Committee consisting of a maximum of nine Members including one Chief, one Deputy Chief, at least three women and at least one Dalit or minority. The District Coordination Committee shall discharge all functions required to be discharged by the District Assembly.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 4
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
A Member of a Village Assembly or Municipal Assembly within the concerned district shall be eligible to be a candidate for the office of Chief, Deputy Chief or Member of the District Coordination Committee. If elected to the office of Chief, Deputy Chief or Member of the District Coordination
Committee, his or her office of Member of Village Assembly or of Municipal Assembly shall ipso facto lapse.
raw_text:
A Member of a Village Assembly or Municipal Assembly within the concerned district shall be eligible to be a candidate for the office of Chief, Deputy Chief or Member of the District Coordination Committee. If elected to the office of Chief, Deputy Chief or Member of the District Coordination
Committee, his or her office of Member of Village Assembly or of Municipal Assembly shall ipso facto lapse.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 5
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
The term of office of the Chief, Deputy Chief and member of the District Coordination Committee shall be five years after the date of being elected.
raw_text: The term of office of the Chief, Deputy Chief and member of the District Coordination Committee shall be five years after the date of being elected.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 6
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
The office of the Chief, Deputy Chief and member of the District Coordination Committee shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the Chief tenders resignation in writing before the Deputy Chief, and if the Deputy Chief or member tenders resignation in writing before the Chief,
(b) if his or her term of office expires,
(c) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Chief, Deputy Chief and member of the District Coordination Committee shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the Chief tenders resignation in writing before the Deputy Chief, and if the Deputy Chief or member tenders resignation in writing before the Chief,
(b) if his or her term of office expires,
(c) if he or she dies.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 7
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
The functions, duties and powers of the District Assembly shall be as follows:
(a) to make coordination between the Village Bodies and Municipalities within the district,
(b) to monitor development and construction works to make balance of such works,
(c) to make coordination between the Federal and the State Government offices and Village Bodies and Municipalities in the district,
(d) to perform other functions as provided for in the State law.
raw_text:
The functions, duties and powers of the District Assembly shall be as follows:
(a) to make coordination between the Village Bodies and Municipalities within the district,
(b) to monitor development and construction works to make balance of such works,
(c) to make coordination between the Federal and the State Government offices and Village Bodies and Municipalities in the district,
(d) to perform other functions as provided for in the State law.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text:
Part 17 Article 220
Clause 8
District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
Provisions relating to the conduct of the District Assembly, facilities of Members of the District Coordination Committee and other matters relating to the District Assembly shall be as provided for in the State law
raw_text: Provisions relating to the conduct of the District Assembly, facilities of Members of the District Coordination Committee and other matters relating to the District Assembly shall be as provided for in the State law

title: Legislative powers of Local Level
text:
Part 18 Article 221
Clause 1
Legislative powers of Local Level
The legislative powers of the Local Level shall be vested in the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, subject to this Constitution.
raw_text: The legislative powers of the Local Level shall be vested in the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, subject to this Constitution.

title: Legislative powers of Local Level
text:
Part 18 Article 221
Clause 2
Legislative powers of Local Level
The legislative powers of the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly shall be as mentioned in the lists contained in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.
raw_text: The legislative powers of the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly shall be as mentioned in the lists contained in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 1
Composition of Village Assembly
Each Village Body shall have a Village Assembly.
raw_text: Each Village Body shall have a Village Assembly.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 2
Composition of Village Assembly
A Village Assembly under clause (1) shall consist of the Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of the Village Executive, Ward Chairpersons, and four members elected from each ward and Members of the Village Executive elected from Dalit and minority communities pursuant to clause (4) of Article 215.
raw_text: A Village Assembly under clause (1) shall consist of the Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of the Village Executive, Ward Chairpersons, and four members elected from each ward and Members of the Village Executive elected from Dalit and minority communities pursuant to clause (4) of Article 215.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 3
Composition of Village Assembly
A Village Assembly to be formed under clause (1) shall have representation of at least two women from each ward.
raw_text: A Village Assembly to be formed under clause (1) shall have representation of at least two women from each ward.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 4
Composition of Village Assembly
Each ward of a Village Body under the Federal law shall have a Ward Committee composed of the Ward Chairperson and four members. Such Ward Chairperson and Ward members shall be elected in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.
raw_text: Each ward of a Village Body under the Federal law shall have a Ward Committee composed of the Ward Chairperson and four members. Such Ward Chairperson and Ward members shall be elected in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 5
Composition of Village Assembly
Every person who has completed the age of eighteen years and whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Village Body shall have a right to vote as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Every person who has completed the age of eighteen years and whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Village Body shall have a right to vote as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 6
Composition of Village Assembly
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to become a candidate for the office of the Member of the Village Assembly:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being his or her name included in the electoral rolls of the Village Body, and
(d) not being disqualified by any law.
raw_text:
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to become a candidate for the office of the Member of the Village Assembly:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being his or her name included in the electoral rolls of the Village Body, and
(d) not being disqualified by any law.

title: Composition of Village Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 222
Clause 7
Composition of Village Assembly
Matters relating to the election to the Village Assembly and other matters pertaining thereto shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Matters relating to the election to the Village Assembly and other matters pertaining thereto shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 1
Composition of Municipal Assembly
Each Municipality shall have a Municipal Assembly.
raw_text: Each Municipality shall have a Municipal Assembly.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 2
Composition of Municipal Assembly
A Municipal Assembly under clause (1) shall consist of the Mayor and the Deputy Mayor of the Municipal Executive, Ward Chairpersons, and four members elected from each Ward and members of the Municipal Executive elected from Dalit or minority communities pursuant to clause (4) of Article 216.
raw_text: A Municipal Assembly under clause (1) shall consist of the Mayor and the Deputy Mayor of the Municipal Executive, Ward Chairpersons, and four members elected from each Ward and members of the Municipal Executive elected from Dalit or minority communities pursuant to clause (4) of Article 216.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 3
Composition of Municipal Assembly
A Municipal Assembly to be formed under clause (1) shall have representation of at least two women from each Ward.
raw_text: A Municipal Assembly to be formed under clause (1) shall have representation of at least two women from each Ward.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 4
Composition of Municipal Assembly
Each ward of a Municipality shall have a Ward Committee composed of one Ward Chairperson and four members as provided for in the Federal law. Such Ward Chairperson and Ward members shall be elected in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.
raw_text: Each ward of a Municipality shall have a Ward Committee composed of one Ward Chairperson and four members as provided for in the Federal law. Such Ward Chairperson and Ward members shall be elected in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 5
Composition of Municipal Assembly
Every person who has completed the age of eighteen years and whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality shall have a right to vote as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Every person who has completed the age of eighteen years and whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality shall have a right to vote as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 6
Composition of Municipal Assembly
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to be a candidate for the office of member of the Municipal Assembly:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being his or her name included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality, and
(d) not being disqualified by any law.
raw_text:
A person who has the following qualification shall be qualified to be a candidate for the office of member of the Municipal Assembly:
(a) being a citizen of Nepal,
(b) having completed the age of twenty one years,
(c) being his or her name included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality, and
(d) not being disqualified by any law.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 223
Clause 7
Composition of Municipal Assembly
Matters relating to the election to the Municipal Assembly and other matters pertaining thereto shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Matters relating to the election to the Municipal Assembly and other matters pertaining thereto shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 224
Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
The Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of a Village Executive and the Mayor and Deputy Mayor of a Municipal Executive shall ipso facto be the Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, respectively, and perform functions as such.
raw_text: The Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of a Village Executive and the Mayor and Deputy Mayor of a Municipal Executive shall ipso facto be the Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, respectively, and perform functions as such.

title: Term of Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 225
Term of Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
The term of a Village Assembly and of a Municipal Assembly shall be five years for the date of election. Another Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly shall be elected not later than six months of the expiration of such a term.
raw_text: The term of a Village Assembly and of a Municipal Assembly shall be five years for the date of election. Another Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly shall be elected not later than six months of the expiration of such a term.

title: Powers to make law
text:
Part 18 Article 226
Clause 1
Powers to make law
A Village Assembly and a Municipal Assembly may make necessary laws on the matters set forth in the Lists contained in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.
raw_text: A Village Assembly and a Municipal Assembly may make necessary laws on the matters set forth in the Lists contained in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.

title: Powers to make law
text:
Part 18 Article 226
Clause 2
Powers to make law
The process for making laws under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the State Law.
raw_text: The process for making laws under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the State Law.

title: Other provisions relating to Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
text:
Part 18 Article 227
Other provisions relating to Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
Other matters relating to the conduct of business of a Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly, rules of procedures of meetings, formation of committees, conditions in which the office of member falls vacant, facilities receivable by members of the Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly and employees and offices of the Village Body and Municipality shall be as provided for in the State law
raw_text: Other matters relating to the conduct of business of a Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly, rules of procedures of meetings, formation of committees, conditions in which the office of member falls vacant, facilities receivable by members of the Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly and employees and offices of the Village Body and Municipality shall be as provided for in the State law

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text:
Part 19 Article 228
Clause 1
No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
No tax shall be levied and collected and no loan raised in the Local level except in accordance with law.
raw_text: No tax shall be levied and collected and no loan raised in the Local level except in accordance with law.

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text:
Part 19 Article 228
Clause 2
No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
The Local level may levy tax by law on matters falling within its domain without prejudice to national economic policies, carriage of goods and services, capital and labour market, and the neighbouring State or Local level.
raw_text: The Local level may levy tax by law on matters falling within its domain without prejudice to national economic policies, carriage of goods and services, capital and labour market, and the neighbouring State or Local level.

title: Local Consolidated Fund
text:
Part 19 Article 229
Clause 1
Local Consolidated Fund
Each Village Body and Municipality under the Local Level shall have one Local Consolidate Fund. All revenues received by the Village Body or Municipality, any amounts of grants received from the Government of Nepal and the State Government, all loans raised by the Village Body and Municipality, and amounts received from other sources shall be credited to such a Fund.
raw_text: Each Village Body and Municipality under the Local Level shall have one Local Consolidate Fund. All revenues received by the Village Body or Municipality, any amounts of grants received from the Government of Nepal and the State Government, all loans raised by the Village Body and Municipality, and amounts received from other sources shall be credited to such a Fund.

title: Local Consolidated Fund
text:
Part 19 Article 229
Clause 2
Local Consolidated Fund
Matters relating to expenditures from the Local Consolidated Fund under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the Local law.
raw_text: Matters relating to expenditures from the Local Consolidated Fund under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the Local law.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
text:
Part 19 Article 230
Clause 1
Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
The Village Executive and the Municipal Executive shall, subject to this Constitution, lay an estimate of revenues and expenditures of every financial year before, and have the estimate passed by, the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, respectively, as provided for in the Local law.
raw_text: The Village Executive and the Municipal Executive shall, subject to this Constitution, lay an estimate of revenues and expenditures of every financial year before, and have the estimate passed by, the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, respectively, as provided for in the Local law.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
text:
Part 19 Article 230
Clause 2
Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
If, in laying an estimate of revenues and expenditures under clause (1), the Village Executive or Municipal Executive is required to make a deficit budget, it must purpose also the sources to meet the deficit as provided for in the Federal law and the State law
raw_text: If, in laying an estimate of revenues and expenditures under clause (1), the Village Executive or Municipal Executive is required to make a deficit budget, it must purpose also the sources to meet the deficit as provided for in the Federal law and the State law

title: Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
text:
Part 20 Article 231
Clause 1
Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
The Federal Law may be made to be applicable to the whole of, or, if required, to any part of, the territory of Nepal.
raw_text: The Federal Law may be made to be applicable to the whole of, or, if required, to any part of, the territory of Nepal.

title: Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
text:
Part 20 Article 231
Clause 2
Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
A State law may be made to be applicable to the whole of, or as required, to any part of the territory of the State.
raw_text: A State law may be made to be applicable to the whole of, or as required, to any part of the territory of the State.

title: Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
text:
Part 20 Article 231
Clause 3
Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
If two or more States make a request to the Government of Nepal to make laws on any matter enumerated in Schedule-6, the Federal Parliament may make necessary laws. Such laws shall be applicable only to the concerned States.
raw_text: If two or more States make a request to the Government of Nepal to make laws on any matter enumerated in Schedule-6, the Federal Parliament may make necessary laws. Such laws shall be applicable only to the concerned States.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 1
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
The relations between the Federation, States, and Local level shall be based on the principles of cooperation, co-existence and coordination.
raw_text: The relations between the Federation, States, and Local level shall be based on the principles of cooperation, co-existence and coordination.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 2
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
The Government of Nepal may, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal Law, give necessary directions to any State Council of Ministers on matters of national importance and on matters to be coordinated between the States, and it shall be the duty of the concerned State Council of Ministers to abide by such directions.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal may, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal Law, give necessary directions to any State Council of Ministers on matters of national importance and on matters to be coordinated between the States, and it shall be the duty of the concerned State Council of Ministers to abide by such directions.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 3
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
If any such type of act as may seriously undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality or independence of Nepal is carried out in any State, the President may, as required, warn such State Council of Ministers, suspend or dissolve the State Council of Ministers and the State Assembly for a period not exceeding six months.
raw_text: If any such type of act as may seriously undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality or independence of Nepal is carried out in any State, the President may, as required, warn such State Council of Ministers, suspend or dissolve the State Council of Ministers and the State Assembly for a period not exceeding six months.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 4
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
Any suspension or dissolution of any State Council of Ministers and State Assembly made pursuant to clause (3) must get ratified by a two-
thirds majority of the total number of the then members of the Federal Parliament within thirty five days.
raw_text:
Any suspension or dissolution of any State Council of Ministers and State Assembly made pursuant to clause (3) must get ratified by a two-
thirds majority of the total number of the then members of the Federal Parliament within thirty five days.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 5
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
If dissolution made pursuant to clause (3) is ratified by the Federal Parliament, election to the State Assembly shall be held in such State within six months.
Provided that such suspension or dissolution shall ipso facto be invalid if it is not ratified by the Federal Parliament.
raw_text:
If dissolution made pursuant to clause (3) is ratified by the Federal Parliament, election to the State Assembly shall be held in such State within six months.
Provided that such suspension or dissolution shall ipso facto be invalid if it is not ratified by the Federal Parliament.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 6
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
The Federal ruling shall apply to such State during the period of such suspension if the suspension made pursuant to clause (3) is ratified pursuant to clause (4) and until election to the State Assembly is held pursuant to clause (5).
raw_text: The Federal ruling shall apply to such State during the period of such suspension if the suspension made pursuant to clause (3) is ratified pursuant to clause (4) and until election to the State Assembly is held pursuant to clause (5).

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text:
Part 20 Article 232
Clause 7
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
During the continuance of the Federal rule, the Federal Parliament may make laws with respect to any matter enumerated in the List contained in Schedule-6.
raw_text: During the continuance of the Federal rule, the Federal Parliament may make laws with respect to any matter enumerated in the List contained in Schedule-6.

title: Relations between States
text:
Part 20 Article 233
Clause 1
Relations between States
One State shall render assistance in the execution of legal provisions or judicial and administrative decisions or orders of another State.
raw_text: One State shall render assistance in the execution of legal provisions or judicial and administrative decisions or orders of another State.

title: Relations between States
text:
Part 20 Article 233
Clause 2
Relations between States
A State may exchange information and consult with another State on matters of common concern and interest, coordinate each other on their activities and legislations and extend mutual assistance.
raw_text: A State may exchange information and consult with another State on matters of common concern and interest, coordinate each other on their activities and legislations and extend mutual assistance.

title: Relations between States
text:
Part 20 Article 233
Clause 3
Relations between States
A State shall, in accordance with its State law, provide equal security, treatment and facility to residents of another State.
raw_text: A State shall, in accordance with its State law, provide equal security, treatment and facility to residents of another State.

title: Inter-State Council
text:
Part 20 Article 234
Clause 1
Inter-State Council
There shall be an Inter-State Council as follows to settle political disputes arising between the Federation and a State and between States:
(a) Prime Minister - Chairperson
(b) Minister for Home Affairs of the Government of Nepal    - Member
(c) Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal    - Member
(d) Chief Ministers of the concerned States - Member
raw_text:
There shall be an Inter-State Council as follows to settle political disputes arising between the Federation and a State and between States:
(a) Prime Minister - Chairperson
(b) Minister for Home Affairs of the Government of Nepal    - Member
(c) Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal    - Member
(d) Chief Ministers of the concerned States - Member

title: Inter-State Council
text:
Part 20 Article 234
Clause 2
Inter-State Council
The Inter-State Council may meet as required.
raw_text: The Inter-State Council may meet as required.

title: Inter-State Council
text:
Part 20 Article 234
Clause 3
Inter-State Council
The Inter-State Council may invite to its meeting a Minister of the Government of Nepal and a Minister of the concerned State who is responsible for the matter of dispute and a concerned expert.
raw_text: The Inter-State Council may invite to its meeting a Minister of the Government of Nepal and a Minister of the concerned State who is responsible for the matter of dispute and a concerned expert.

title: Inter-State Council
text:
Part 20 Article 234
Clause 4
Inter-State Council
The rules of procedures of the meeting of the Inter-State Council shall be as determined by the Council itself.
raw_text: The rules of procedures of the meeting of the Inter-State Council shall be as determined by the Council itself.

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text:
Part 20 Article 235
Clause 1
Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
The Federal Parliament shall make necessary laws in order to maintain coordination between the Federation, State and Local level.
raw_text: The Federal Parliament shall make necessary laws in order to maintain coordination between the Federation, State and Local level.

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text:
Part 20 Article 235
Clause 2
Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
The State Assembly may maintain coordination between the State and Village Bodies or Municipalities and settle political disputes, if any, that have arisen, in coordination with the concerned Village Body, Municipality and the District Coordination Committee.

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text:
Part 20 Article 235
Clause 3
Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
The processes and procedures for the settlement of disputes under clause (2) shall be as provided for in the State law.
raw_text: The processes and procedures for the settlement of disputes under clause (2) shall be as provided for in the State law.

title: Inter-State trade
text:
Part 20 Article 236
Inter-State trade
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Constitution, it is prohibited to make any kind of obstruction to the carriage of
goods or extension of services by a State or Local level to another State or Local level or to the carriage of goods or extension of services to any State or Local Level or to levy tax, fee or charge thereon or to make any kind of discrimination on the carriage or extension of such services or goods.
raw_text:
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Constitution, it is prohibited to make any kind of obstruction to the carriage of
goods or extension of services by a State or Local level to another State or Local level or to the carriage of goods or extension of services to any State or Local Level or to levy tax, fee or charge thereon or to make any kind of discrimination on the carriage or extension of such services or goods.

title: Not to affect jurisdiction of Constitutional Bench of Supreme Court
text:
Part 20 Article 237
Not to affect jurisdiction of Constitutional Bench of Supreme Court
Nothing contained in this Part shall affect the jurisdiction of the Constitutional Bench of the Supreme Court under Article 137
raw_text: Nothing contained in this Part shall affect the jurisdiction of the Constitutional Bench of the Supreme Court under Article 137

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 1
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
There shall be a Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority of Nepal, consisting of the Chief Commissioner and four other Commissioners. The Chief Commissioner shall act as the Chairperson of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority.
raw_text: There shall be a Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority of Nepal, consisting of the Chief Commissioner and four other Commissioners. The Chief Commissioner shall act as the Chairperson of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 2
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
The President shall, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Chief Commissioner and Commissioners.
raw_text: The President shall, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Chief Commissioner and Commissioners.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 3
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
The term of office of the Chief Commissioner and Commissioners shall be six years from the date of appointment.
raw_text: The term of office of the Chief Commissioner and Commissioners shall be six years from the date of appointment.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 4
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
Notwithstanding anything contained in clause (3), the office of the Chief Commissioner or a Commissioner shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,
(b) if he or she attains the age of sixty-five years,
(c) if a motion of impeachment is passed against him or her under Article 101,
(d) if he or she is removed from office by the President on recommendation of the Constitutional Council on grounds of his or her inability to hold office and discharge the functions due to physical or mental illness,
(e) if he or she dies.

(e) if he or she dies.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 5
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
The Chief Commissioner and Commissioners appointed under clause (2) shall not be eligible for reappointment.
Provided that a Commissioner may be appointed to the office of Chief Commissioner, and when a Commissioner is so appointed as the Chief Commissioner, his or her term of office shall be so computed as to include his or her term as the Commissioner.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 6
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
A person shall be eligible to be appointed as the Chief Commissioner or a Commissioner of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority if he or she possesses the following qualification:
(a) holding a bachelor's degree from a recognized university,
(b) not being a member of any political party at the time of appointment,
(c) having gained at least twenty years of experiences in the field of either accounting, revenue, engineering, law, development or research and being a distinguished person,
(d) having attained the age of forty five years, and
(e) being of high moral character.

(e) being of high moral character.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 7
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Commissioner and the Commissioners shall be as provided for in the Federal law. The remuneration and conditions of service of the Chief Commissioner and the Commissioners shall not, so long as they hold office, be altered to their disadvantage.
Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 238
Clause 8
Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
A person once appointed as the Chief Commissioner or Commissioner shall not be eligible for appointment in other government service.
Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 239
Clause 1
Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
The Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may, in accordance with law, conduct, or cause to be conducted, investigations of any abuse of authority committed through corruption by any person holding public office.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to any official in relation to whom this Constitution itself separately provides for such action and to any official in relation to whom other law provides for a separate special provision.
raw_text:
The Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may, in accordance with law, conduct, or cause to be conducted, investigations of any abuse of authority committed through corruption by any person holding public office.
Provided that this clause shall not apply to any official in relation to whom this Constitution itself separately provides for such action and to any official in relation to whom other law provides for a separate special provision.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 239
Clause 2
Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
In the case of the persons who can be removed from office by passing a motion of impeachment under Article 101, the Judges who can be removed by the Judicial Council and the persons who are liable to action under the Army Act, it may conduct, or cause to be conducted, investigations in accordance with the Federal law after they have been removed from office.
raw_text: In the case of the persons who can be removed from office by passing a motion of impeachment under Article 101, the Judges who can be removed by the Judicial Council and the persons who are liable to action under the Army Act, it may conduct, or cause to be conducted, investigations in accordance with the Federal law after they have been removed from office.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 239
Clause 3
Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
If the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority finds, on investigation conducted pursuant to clause (1) or (2), that a person holding public office has committed an act which is defined by law as corruption, it may file, or cause to be filed, a case against that person and other person involved in that offense in the competent court in accordance with law.
raw_text: If the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority finds, on investigation conducted pursuant to clause (1) or (2), that a person holding public office has committed an act which is defined by law as corruption, it may file, or cause to be filed, a case against that person and other person involved in that offense in the competent court in accordance with law.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 239
Clause 4
Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
If, on investigation conducted pursuant to clause (1) or (2), any act or action done or taken by a person holding public office appears to be of such nature as to be falling under the jurisdiction of another official or body,
the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may write to the concerned official or body for necessary action.
raw_text:
If, on investigation conducted pursuant to clause (1) or (2), any act or action done or taken by a person holding public office appears to be of such nature as to be falling under the jurisdiction of another official or body,
the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may write to the concerned official or body for necessary action.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text:
Part 21 Article 239
Clause 5
Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
The Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may delegate any of its functions, duties and powers on the conducting of investigations or filing cases, to the Chief Commissioner, a Commissioner or an officer employee of the Government of Nepal to be exercised and complied with subject to the specified conditions.

Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority shall be as provided for in the Federal law

text:

Clause 5
Auditor General

title: Auditor General
text:
Part 22 Article 240
Clause 6
Auditor General
A person shall be eligible to be appointed as the Auditor General if he or she possesses the following qualification:
(a) having served in the Special Class of the Government of Nepal or having at least twenty years of experiences in audit related work, after having obtained a bachelor's
degree in management, commerce or accounting from a recognized university or having passed a chartered accountancy examination,
(b) not being a member of any political party at the time of appointment,
(c) having attained the age of forty five years, and
(d) being of high moral character.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text:
Part 22 Article 241
Clause 1
Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
The accounts of all Federal and State Government Offices including the Office of the President, Office of the Vice-President, Supreme Court, Federal Parliament, State Assembly, State Government, Local level, Constitutional Bodies and Offices thereof, Courts, Office of the Attorney General, Nepal Army, Nepal Police and Armed Police Force, Nepal shall be audited by the Auditor-General in
accordance with law, having regard to, inter alia, the regularity, economy, efficiency, effectiveness and the propriety thereof.

The accounts of all Federal and State Government Offices including the Office of the President, Office of the Vice-President, Supreme Court, Federal Parliament, State Assembly, State Government, Local level, Constitutional Bodies and Offices thereof, Courts, Office of the Attorney General, Nepal Army, Nepal Police and Armed Police Force, Nepal shall be audited by the Auditor-General in
accordance with law, having regard to, inter alia, the regularity, economy, efficiency, effectiveness and the propriety thereof.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text:
Part 22 Article 241
Clause 2
Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
The Auditor General shall be consulted in the matter of appointment of an auditor to carry out the audit of a corporate body of which the Government of Nepal or State Government owns more than fifty percent of the shares or assets. The Auditor General may also issue necessary directives setting forth the principles for carrying out the audit of such corporate body.
raw_text: The Auditor General shall be consulted in the matter of appointment of an auditor to carry out the audit of a corporate body of which the Government of Nepal or State Government owns more than fifty percent of the shares or assets. The Auditor General may also issue necessary directives setting forth the principles for carrying out the audit of such corporate body.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text:
Part 22 Article 241
Clause 3
Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
The Auditor General shall, at all times, have power to examine any books of accounts for the purpose of carrying out the functions under clause (1). It shall be the duty of the concerned chief of office to provide all such documents and information as may be demanded by the Auditor General or any of his or her employees.

It shall be the duty of the concerned chief of office to provide all such documents and information as may be demanded by the Auditor General or any of his or her employees.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text:
Part 22 Article 241
Clause 4
Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
The accounts to be audited pursuant to clause (1) shall be maintained in the form prescribed by the Auditor General, as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: The accounts to be audited pursuant to clause (1) shall be maintained in the form prescribed by the Auditor General, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text:
Part 22 Article 241
Clause 5
Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
In addition to the accounts of the offices mentioned in clause (1), the Federal law may also require the accounts of any other offices or bodies to be audited by the Auditor General
raw_text: In addition to the accounts of the offices mentioned in clause (1), the Federal law may also require the accounts of any other offices or bodies to be audited by the Auditor General

title: Public Service Commission
text:
Part 23 Article 242
Clause 1
Public Service Commission
There shall be a Public Service Commission of Nepal, consisting of the Chairperson and four other Members.
raw_text: There shall be a Public Service Commission of Nepal, consisting of the Chairperson and four other Members.

title: Public Service Commission
text:

Clause 2
Public Service Commission
The President shall, on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Chairperson and Members.

title: Public Service Commission
text:
Part 23 Article 242
Clause 3
Public Service Commission
At least fifty percent of the total number of Members of the Public Service Commission shall be appointed from amongst the persons who have worked for twenty or more in any government service, and the rest of the Members shall be appointed from amongst the persons who hold reputation after having done research, investigation, teaching or any other significant work in the field of science, technology, art, literature, law, public administration, sociology or other sphere of national life.

title: Public Service Commission
text:
Part 23 Article 242
Clause 4
