title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
text:
Part 32 Article 284
Clause 5
Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
The Chief Secretary of the Government of Nepal shall act as the secretary of the Constitutional Council.
raw_text: The Chief Secretary of the Government of Nepal shall act as the secretary of the Constitutional Council.

title: Constitution of government service
text:
Part 32 Article 285
Clause 1
Constitution of government service
The Government of Nepal may, in order to run the administration of the country, constitute the Federal civil service and such other Federal government services as may be required. The constitution, operation and conditions of service of such services shall be as provided for in the Federal Act.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal may, in order to run the administration of the country, constitute the Federal civil service and such other Federal government services as may be required. The constitution, operation and conditions of service of such services shall be as provided for in the Federal Act.

title: Constitution of government service
text:
Part 32 Article 285
Clause 2
Constitution of government service
Positions in the Federal civil service as well as all Federal government services shall be filled through competitive examinations, on the basis of open and proportional inclusive principle.
raw_text: Positions in the Federal civil service as well as all Federal government services shall be filled through competitive examinations, on the basis of open and proportional inclusive principle.

title: Constitution of government service
text:
Part 32 Article 285
Clause 3
Constitution of government service
The State Council of Ministers, Village Executives and Municipal Executives may by law constitute and operate various government services as required for the operation of their administration.
raw_text: The State Council of Ministers, Village Executives and Municipal Executives may by law constitute and operate various government services as required for the operation of their administration.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 1
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The Government of Nepal may constitute an Election Constituency Delimitation Commission, to
determine election constituencies for the purpose of election to the members of the Federal Parliament and members of the State Assemblies in accordance with this Constitution, which shall consist of the following as the Chairperson and members:
(a) Retired Judge of the Supreme Court                 -Chairperson
(b) One Geographer -Member
(c) One sociologist or demographer                        -Member
(d) One administration expert or jurist                    -Member
(e) Gazetted special class officer of the Government of Nepal -Member- secretary
raw_text:
The Government of Nepal may constitute an Election Constituency Delimitation Commission, to
determine election constituencies for the purpose of election to the members of the Federal Parliament and members of the State Assemblies in accordance with this Constitution, which shall consist of the following as the Chairperson and members:
(a) Retired Judge of the Supreme Court                 -Chairperson
(b) One Geographer -Member
(c) One sociologist or demographer                        -Member
(d) One administration expert or jurist                    -Member
(e) Gazetted special class officer of the Government of Nepal -Member- secretary

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 2
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The term of office of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be as prescribed at the time of its constitution.
raw_text: The term of office of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be as prescribed at the time of its constitution.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 3
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
A person shall be qualified to be appointed as the Chairperson or a member of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission if he or she possesses the following qualification:
(a) holding at least bachelor’s degree in related subject from a recognized university,
(b) having attained the age of forty-five years, and
(c) having high moral character.
raw_text:
A person shall be qualified to be appointed as the Chairperson or a member of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission if he or she possesses the following qualification:
(a) holding at least bachelor’s degree in related subject from a recognized university,
(b) having attained the age of forty-five years, and
(c) having high moral character.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 4
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The office of the Chairperson or a member of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing,
(b) if he or she is removed by the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers,
(c) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Chairperson or a member of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing,
(b) if he or she is removed by the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers,
(c) if he or she dies.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 5
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
While determining election constituencies pursuant to this Article, the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall so determine the constituencies, having regard to population and geography as the basis of representation, and as to ensure that the ratio between the geography, population and number of members of such election constituencies is equal, so far as practicable.
raw_text: While determining election constituencies pursuant to this Article, the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall so determine the constituencies, having regard to population and geography as the basis of representation, and as to ensure that the ratio between the geography, population and number of members of such election constituencies is equal, so far as practicable.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 6
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
While delimitating election constituencies pursuant to clause (5), regard must be had to, inter alia, the density of population, geographical specificity, administrative and transportation convenience, community and cultural aspects of the constituencies.
raw_text: While delimitating election constituencies pursuant to clause (5), regard must be had to, inter alia, the density of population, geographical specificity, administrative and transportation convenience, community and cultural aspects of the constituencies.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 7
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
No question may be raised in any court on any matter of the determination, or review, of election constituencies made by the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission.
raw_text: No question may be raised in any court on any matter of the determination, or review, of election constituencies made by the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 8
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall submit a report on the works it has performed to the Government of Nepal.
raw_text: The Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall submit a report on the works it has performed to the Government of Nepal.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 9
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers, shall lay the report received pursuant to clause (8) before the Federal Parliament and send it to the Election Commission for implementation.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers, shall lay the report received pursuant to clause (8) before the Federal Parliament and send it to the Election Commission for implementation.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 10
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall determine its rules of procedures itself.
raw_text: The Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall determine its rules of procedures itself.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 11
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The remuneration and facilities of the Chairperson and the members of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be similar to those of the Chief Election Commissioner and the Election Commissioners of the Election Commission, respectively.
raw_text: The remuneration and facilities of the Chairperson and the members of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be similar to those of the Chief Election Commissioner and the Election Commissioners of the Election Commission, respectively.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 12
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The election constituencies delimited in accordance with clause (5) must be reviewed in every twenty years.
raw_text: The election constituencies delimited in accordance with clause (5) must be reviewed in every twenty years.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 286
Clause 13
Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
The Government of Nepal shall provide such employees as may be required for the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal shall provide such employees as may be required for the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 1
Language Commission
The Government of Nepal shall, no later than one year of the commencement of this Constitution, constitute a Language Commission comprising representation of States.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal shall, no later than one year of the commencement of this Constitution, constitute a Language Commission comprising representation of States.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 2
Language Commission
The Language Commission shall consist of one Chairperson and a required number of members.
raw_text: The Language Commission shall consist of one Chairperson and a required number of members.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 3
Language Commission
The term of office of the Chairperson and members of the Language Commission shall be six years from the date of their appointment. They may not be reappointed.
raw_text: The term of office of the Chairperson and members of the Language Commission shall be six years from the date of their appointment. They may not be reappointed.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 4
Language Commission
A person shall be qualified to be appointed as the Chairperson or a member of the Language Commission if he or she possesses the following qualification:
(a) holding master’s degree in related subject from a recognized university,
(b) having gained at least twenty years of experience in the fields of study, teaching and research on various languages of Nepal,
(c) having completed the age of forty-five years, and
(d) having high moral character.
raw_text:
A person shall be qualified to be appointed as the Chairperson or a member of the Language Commission if he or she possesses the following qualification:
(a) holding master’s degree in related subject from a recognized university,
(b) having gained at least twenty years of experience in the fields of study, teaching and research on various languages of Nepal,
(c) having completed the age of forty-five years, and
(d) having high moral character.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 5
Language Commission
The office of the Chairperson or a member of the Language Commission shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing,
(b) if he or she is removed by the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers,
(c) if he completes the age  of sixty five years,
(d) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Chairperson or a member of the Language Commission shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing,
(b) if he or she is removed by the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers,
(c) if he completes the age  of sixty five years,
(d) if he or she dies.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 6
Language Commission
The functions, duties and powers of the Language Commission shall be as follows:
(a) to determine the criteria to be fulfilled for the recognition of the official language and make recommendations on languages to the Government of Nepal,
(b) to make recommendations to the Government of Nepal, on the measures to be adopted for the protection, promotion and development of languages,
(c) to measure the levels of development of mother tongues and make suggestions to the Government of Nepal, on the potentiality of their use in education,
(d) to study, research and monitor languages.
raw_text:
The functions, duties and powers of the Language Commission shall be as follows:
(a) to determine the criteria to be fulfilled for the recognition of the official language and make recommendations on languages to the Government of Nepal,
(b) to make recommendations to the Government of Nepal, on the measures to be adopted for the protection, promotion and development of languages,
(c) to measure the levels of development of mother tongues and make suggestions to the Government of Nepal, on the potentiality of their use in education,
(d) to study, research and monitor languages.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 7
Language Commission
The Language Commission shall complete its task under sub-clause (a) of clause (6) no later than five years after the date of its constitution.
raw_text: The Language Commission shall complete its task under sub-clause (a) of clause (6) no later than five years after the date of its constitution.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 8
Language Commission
The Government of Nepal may, in coordination with a State Government, establish a branch of the Language Commission in that State.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal may, in coordination with a State Government, establish a branch of the Language Commission in that State.

title: Language Commission
text:
Part 32 Article 287
Clause 9
Language Commission
The other functions, duties, powers and rules of procedures of the Language Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: The other functions, duties, powers and rules of procedures of the Language Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Capital
text:
Part 32 Article 288
Clause 1
Capital
The capital of Nepal shall be situated in Kathmandu.
raw_text: The capital of Nepal shall be situated in Kathmandu.

title: Capital
text:
Part 32 Article 288
Clause 2
Capital
The capital of a State under this Constitution shall be as decided by a two-thirds majority of the number of the then members of the concerned State Assembly.
raw_text: The capital of a State under this Constitution shall be as decided by a two-thirds majority of the number of the then members of the concerned State Assembly.

title: Capital
text:
Part 32 Article 288
Clause 3
Capital
The business of a State shall be conducted through the place as specified by the Government of Nepal until decision is made in accordance with clause (2).
raw_text: The business of a State shall be conducted through the place as specified by the Government of Nepal until decision is made in accordance with clause (2).

title: Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
text:
Part 32 Article 289
Clause 1
Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
In order for a person to be elected, nominated or appointed to the office of President, Vice-President, Prime Minister, Chief Justice, Speaker of the House of Representatives, Chief of State, Chief Minister, Speaker of a State Assembly and chief of a security body, the person must have obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent.
raw_text: In order for a person to be elected, nominated or appointed to the office of President, Vice-President, Prime Minister, Chief Justice, Speaker of the House of Representatives, Chief of State, Chief Minister, Speaker of a State Assembly and chief of a security body, the person must have obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent.

title: Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
text:
Part 32 Article 289
Clause 2
Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
A person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent, a person who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal or a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth shall also be qualified for the office of a constitutional body other than that mentioned in clause (1).
Provided that the person must have resided in Nepal for at least ten years in the case of a person who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal, and for at least five years in the case of a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth or who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal in accordance with clause (6) of Article 11.
raw_text:
A person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent, a person who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal or a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth shall also be qualified for the office of a constitutional body other than that mentioned in clause (1).
Provided that the person must have resided in Nepal for at least ten years in the case of a person who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal, and for at least five years in the case of a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth or who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal in accordance with clause (6) of Article 11.

title: Provisions relating to Guthi (trusts)
text:
Part 32 Article 290
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Guthi (trusts)
The Federal Parliament shall make necessary laws in relation to the rights of the trust and the farmers enjoying possessory rights over trust lands in a manner not to be prejudicial to the basic norms of the trusts.
raw_text: The Federal Parliament shall make necessary laws in relation to the rights of the trust and the farmers enjoying possessory rights over trust lands in a manner not to be prejudicial to the basic norms of the trusts.

title: Provisions relating to Guthi (trusts)
text:
Part 32 Article 290
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Guthi (trusts)
Other matters relating to trusts shall be as provided by the federal law.
raw_text: Other matters relating to trusts shall be as provided by the federal law.

title: Not to be qualified for appointment
text:
Part 32 Article 291
Clause 1
Not to be qualified for appointment
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Constitution, a citizen of Nepal who has obtained a foreign permanent residence permit shall not be qualified for election, nomination or appointment to an office to be elected, nominated or appointed pursuant to this Constitution.
Provided that nothing shall prevent electing, nominating or appointing a person who has renounced such foreign permanent residence permit to such office after the expiry of a period of at least three months.
raw_text:
Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Constitution, a citizen of Nepal who has obtained a foreign permanent residence permit shall not be qualified for election, nomination or appointment to an office to be elected, nominated or appointed pursuant to this Constitution.
Provided that nothing shall prevent electing, nominating or appointing a person who has renounced such foreign permanent residence permit to such office after the expiry of a period of at least three months.

title: Not to be qualified for appointment
text:
Part 32 Article 291
Clause 2
Not to be qualified for appointment
Other matters relating to the citizens of Nepal who have obtained foreign permanent residence permits as mentioned in clause shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Other matters relating to the citizens of Nepal who have obtained foreign permanent residence permits as mentioned in clause shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text:
Part 32 Article 292
Clause 1
Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
Parliamentary hearings shall be conducted as to appointments to the offices of the Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court, members of the Judicial Council, chiefs and members of Constitutional Bodies, who are appointed on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council under this Constitution, and to the offices of ambassadors, as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: Parliamentary hearings shall be conducted as to appointments to the offices of the Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court, members of the Judicial Council, chiefs and members of Constitutional Bodies, who are appointed on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council under this Constitution, and to the offices of ambassadors, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text:
Part 32 Article 292
Clause 2
Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
For the purposes of clause (1), there shall be formed a fifteen- member joint committee consisting of members of both Houses of the Federal Parliament, in accordance with the Federal law.
raw_text: For the purposes of clause (1), there shall be formed a fifteen- member joint committee consisting of members of both Houses of the Federal Parliament, in accordance with the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text:
Part 32 Article 292
Clause 3
Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
No member of the joint committee under clause (2) shall practice law in the Supreme Court during that term of the Federal Parliament.
raw_text: No member of the joint committee under clause (2) shall practice law in the Supreme Court during that term of the Federal Parliament.

title: Monitoring of functioning of Constitutional Bodies
text:
Part 32 Article 293
Clause 1
Monitoring of functioning of Constitutional Bodies
The chiefs and officials of the Constitutional Bodies must be accountable and responsible to the Federal Parliament. The committees of the House of Representatives may monitor and evaluate the functioning, including reports, of the Constitutional Bodies, other than the National Human Rights Commission, and give necessary direction or advice.
raw_text: The chiefs and officials of the Constitutional Bodies must be accountable and responsible to the Federal Parliament. The committees of the House of Representatives may monitor and evaluate the functioning, including reports, of the Constitutional Bodies, other than the National Human Rights Commission, and give necessary direction or advice.

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
text:
Part 32 Article 294
Clause 1
Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
Every Constitutional Body under this Constitution shall submit an annual report of its functioning to the President, and the President shall cause that report to be laid through the Prime Minister before the Federal Parliament.
raw_text: Every Constitutional Body under this Constitution shall submit an annual report of its functioning to the President, and the President shall cause that report to be laid through the Prime Minister before the Federal Parliament.

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
text:
Part 32 Article 294
Clause 2
Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
The matters to be set out in the annual report under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
raw_text: The matters to be set out in the annual report under clause (1) shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
text:
Part 32 Article 294
Clause 3
Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
Notwithstanding anything contained in clause (1), a Constitutional Body may prepare a separate report in relation to the functioning of each State and submit it to the Chief of State
raw_text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause (1), a Constitutional Body may prepare a separate report in relation to the functioning of each State and submit it to the Chief of State

title: Constitution of Federal Commission
text:
Part 33 Article 295
Clause 1
Constitution of Federal Commission
The Government of Nepal may constitute a Federal Commission for making suggestions on matters relating to the boundaries of States.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal may constitute a Federal Commission for making suggestions on matters relating to the boundaries of States.

title: Constitution of Federal Commission
text:
Part 33 Article 295
Clause 2
Constitution of Federal Commission
The names of the States under clause (3) of Article 56 shall be set by a two thirds majority of the total number of members of the concerned State Assemblies.
raw_text: The names of the States under clause (3) of Article 56 shall be set by a two thirds majority of the total number of members of the concerned State Assemblies.

title: Constitution of Federal Commission
text:
Part 33 Article 295
Clause 3
Constitution of Federal Commission
The Government of Nepal shall constitute a commission for the determination of the number and boundaries of Village Bodies, Municipalities and Special, Protected or Autonomous Regions to be formed under clauses (4) and (5) of Article 56
raw_text: The Government of Nepal shall constitute a commission for the determination of the number and boundaries of Village Bodies, Municipalities and Special, Protected or Autonomous Regions to be formed under clauses (4) and (5) of Article 56

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text:
Part 33 Article 296
Clause 1
Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
The Constituent Assembly existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall ipso facto be converted into the Legislature-Parliament after the commencement of this Constitution, and the term of such Legislature- Parliament shall exist until 7 Magha 2074.
Provided that if an election is held to the House of Representatives set forth in this Constitution prior to the expiration of that term, the Legislature-Parliament shall continue to exist until the day before the day specified for the filing of nominations of candidates for that election.
raw_text:
The Constituent Assembly existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall ipso facto be converted into the Legislature-Parliament after the commencement of this Constitution, and the term of such Legislature- Parliament shall exist until 7 Magha 2074.
Provided that if an election is held to the House of Representatives set forth in this Constitution prior to the expiration of that term, the Legislature-Parliament shall continue to exist until the day before the day specified for the filing of nominations of candidates for that election.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text:
Part 33 Article 296
Clause 2
Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
The Bills under consideration of the Legislature-Parliament at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall ipso facto be transferred to the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1).
raw_text: The Bills under consideration of the Legislature-Parliament at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall ipso facto be transferred to the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1).

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text:
Part 33 Article 296
Clause 3
Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
The Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) shall perform the business required to be performed by the Federal Parliament in accordance with this Constitution until election to the House of Representatives is held pursuant to this Constitution.
raw_text: The Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) shall perform the business required to be performed by the Federal Parliament in accordance with this Constitution until election to the House of Representatives is held pursuant to this Constitution.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text:
Part 33 Article 296
Clause 4
Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
The legislative power of the State Assembly with respect of matters set forth in Schedule-6 shall, upon the commencement of this Constitution, be vested in the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) until the State Assembly is formed. Any law so made shall be inoperative in relation to that State after one year of the date of formation of the State Assembly set forth in this Constitution.
raw_text: The legislative power of the State Assembly with respect of matters set forth in Schedule-6 shall, upon the commencement of this Constitution, be vested in the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) until the State Assembly is formed. Any law so made shall be inoperative in relation to that State after one year of the date of formation of the State Assembly set forth in this Constitution.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text:
Part 33 Article 296
Clause 5
Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
The Legislature-Parliament Secretariat, its Secretary General, Secretary and employees existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall, subject to the conditions of service in force at the time of their appointment, exist in the Federal Parliament Secretariat set forth in this Constitution.
raw_text: The Legislature-Parliament Secretariat, its Secretary General, Secretary and employees existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall, subject to the conditions of service in force at the time of their appointment, exist in the Federal Parliament Secretariat set forth in this Constitution.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text:
Part 33 Article 296
Clause 6
Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
Where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, the President shall summon its session no later than seven days after the date of commencement of this Constitution. The President shall thereafter summon a session of the Legislature-Parliament from time to time.
raw_text: Where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, the President shall summon its session no later than seven days after the date of commencement of this Constitution. The President shall thereafter summon a session of the Legislature-Parliament from time to time.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 1
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
The President and the Vice-President existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to hold their respective offices until other President and Vice-President are elected in accordance with this Article.
raw_text: The President and the Vice-President existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to hold their respective offices until other President and Vice-President are elected in accordance with this Article.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 2
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
The Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) of Article 296 shall, on the basis of political understanding, elect the President and the Vice- President, no later than one month after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution and after the date on which the session is summoned in accordance with clause (6) of Article 296 where the Legislature- Parliament is in recess.
raw_text: The Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) of Article 296 shall, on the basis of political understanding, elect the President and the Vice- President, no later than one month after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution and after the date on which the session is summoned in accordance with clause (6) of Article 296 where the Legislature- Parliament is in recess.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 3
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause (2), the President and the Vice-President must be elected by a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament.
raw_text: In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause (2), the President and the Vice-President must be elected by a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 4
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
If the office of the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with clause (2) or (3) falls vacant for any reason, the President or Vice-President shall be elected by the Legislature-Parliament in accordance with this Article until the Federal Parliament is formed.
raw_text: If the office of the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with clause (2) or (3) falls vacant for any reason, the President or Vice-President shall be elected by the Legislature-Parliament in accordance with this Article until the Federal Parliament is formed.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 5
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
The term of office of the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with this Article shall continue to exist until another President or Vice-President elected by an electoral college set forth in Article 62 assumes office.
raw_text: The term of office of the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with this Article shall continue to exist until another President or Vice-President elected by an electoral college set forth in Article 62 assumes office.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 6
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
The office of the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with this Article shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the President tenders resignation in writing to the Vice- President and the Vice-President, before the President,
(b) if a motion of impeachment against him or her is passed in accordance with clause (7),
(c) if another President or Vice-President elected by an electoral college set forth in Article 62 assumes office,
(d) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with this Article shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if the President tenders resignation in writing to the Vice- President and the Vice-President, before the President,
(b) if a motion of impeachment against him or her is passed in accordance with clause (7),
(c) if another President or Vice-President elected by an electoral college set forth in Article 62 assumes office,
(d) if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text:
Part 33 Article 297
Clause 7
Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
At least one fourth of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) of Article 296 may move a motion of impeachment against the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with this Article on the ground of serious violation of this Constitution and law. If the motion is passed by at least two thirds majority of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament, he or she shall relive of his or her office
raw_text: At least one fourth of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause (1) of Article 296 may move a motion of impeachment against the President or Vice-President elected in accordance with this Article on the ground of serious violation of this Constitution and law. If the motion is passed by at least two thirds majority of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament, he or she shall relive of his or her office

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 1
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Council of Ministers existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the Council of Ministers set forth in clause (2) is formed.
raw_text: The Council of Ministers existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the Council of Ministers set forth in clause (2) is formed.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 2
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Prime Minister shall, on the basis of political understanding, be elected no later than seven days after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and after the date on which the session of the Legislature-Parliament summoned in accordance with clause (6) of Article 286 commences where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess, and a Council of Ministers shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.
raw_text: The Prime Minister shall, on the basis of political understanding, be elected no later than seven days after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and after the date on which the session of the Legislature-Parliament summoned in accordance with clause (6) of Article 286 commences where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess, and a Council of Ministers shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 3
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause (2), the Prime Minister shall be elected by a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament.
raw_text: In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause (2), the Prime Minister shall be elected by a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 4
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The composition and allocation of portfolios of the Council of Ministers to be formed in accordance with this Article shall be made through mutual understanding.
raw_text: The composition and allocation of portfolios of the Council of Ministers to be formed in accordance with this Article shall be made through mutual understanding.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 5
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Council of Ministers to be formed in accordance with this Article shall consist of a Deputy Prime Minister and other Ministers, as required.
raw_text: The Council of Ministers to be formed in accordance with this Article shall consist of a Deputy Prime Minister and other Ministers, as required.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 6
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall, in appointing Ministers in accordance with clause (5), appoint Ministers from amongst the members of the Legislature-Parliament, on the recommendation of the concerned party.
raw_text: The Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall, in appointing Ministers in accordance with clause (5), appoint Ministers from amongst the members of the Legislature-Parliament, on the recommendation of the concerned party.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 7
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Prime Minister and other Ministers appointed in accordance with this Article shall be collectively responsible to the Legislature-Parliament, and the Ministers shall be individually responsible to the Prime Minister and the Legislature- Parliament for the work of their respective Ministries.
raw_text: The Prime Minister and other Ministers appointed in accordance with this Article shall be collectively responsible to the Legislature-Parliament, and the Ministers shall be individually responsible to the Prime Minister and the Legislature- Parliament for the work of their respective Ministries.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 8
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The office of the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,
(b) if a vote of no-confidence is passed against him or her or a vote of confidence is not passed in accordance with clause (14),
(c) if he or she ceases to be a member of the Legislature-Parliament,
(d) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,
(b) if a vote of no-confidence is passed against him or her or a vote of confidence is not passed in accordance with clause (14),
(c) if he or she ceases to be a member of the Legislature-Parliament,
(d) if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 9
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The office of the Deputy Prime Minister, Minister, Minister of State and Assistance Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the Prime Minister,
(b) if the Prime Minister is removed from office in accordance with clause (8),
(c) if he or she is removed from office by the Prime Minister on the recommendation or advice of the concerned party,
(d) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Deputy Prime Minister, Minister, Minister of State and Assistance Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall be vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the Prime Minister,
(b) if the Prime Minister is removed from office in accordance with clause (8),
(c) if he or she is removed from office by the Prime Minister on the recommendation or advice of the concerned party,
(d) if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 10
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
Even though the office of the Prime Minister falls vacant under clause (8), the same Council of Ministers shall continue to act until another Council of Ministers is constituted.
raw_text: Even though the office of the Prime Minister falls vacant under clause (8), the same Council of Ministers shall continue to act until another Council of Ministers is constituted.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 11
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
If the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article dies, the Deputy Prime Minister or senior-most Minister shall act as the Prime Minister until another Prime Minister is appointed.
raw_text: If the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article dies, the Deputy Prime Minister or senior-most Minister shall act as the Prime Minister until another Prime Minister is appointed.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 12
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article may, whenever he or she considers necessary or appropriate to show/clarify that he or she has confidence from the Legislature-Parliament, table a resolution to that effect in the Legislature-Parliament for the vote of confidence.
raw_text: The Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article may, whenever he or she considers necessary or appropriate to show/clarify that he or she has confidence from the Legislature-Parliament, table a resolution to that effect in the Legislature-Parliament for the vote of confidence.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 13
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
At least one-fourth of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament may table in writing a motion of no-confidence against the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article. Provided that a motion of no confidence shall not be tabled more than once in six moths' period against the same Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article.
Provided that a motion of no confidence shall not be tabled more than once in six moths' period against the same Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article.
raw_text:
At least one-fourth of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament may table in writing a motion of no-confidence against the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article. Provided that a motion of no confidence shall not be tabled more than once in six moths' period against the same Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article.
Provided that a motion of no confidence shall not be tabled more than once in six moths' period against the same Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 14
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
A motion tabled under clauses (12) and (13) shall be decided by a majority of the total number of the then members of the Legislature Parliament.
raw_text: A motion tabled under clauses (12) and (13) shall be decided by a majority of the total number of the then members of the Legislature Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text:
Part 33 Article 298
Clause 15
Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
The Government of Nepal shall, after the commencement of this Constitution, exercise the powers of a State until the Council of Ministers of the State set forth in this Constitution is formed.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal shall, after the commencement of this Constitution, exercise the powers of a State until the Council of Ministers of the State set forth in this Constitution is formed.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
The Speaker and Deputy Speaker existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to hold their respective offices until another Speaker and Deputy Speaker is elected pursuant to this Constitution.
raw_text: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to hold their respective offices until another Speaker and Deputy Speaker is elected pursuant to this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
The members of the Legislature-Parliament shall, on the basis of political understanding, elect one Speaker and one Deputy Speaker from themselves no later than twenty days after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and after the date on which the session of the Legislature-Parliament is summoned in accordance with clause (6) of Article 296 where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess.
raw_text: The members of the Legislature-Parliament shall, on the basis of political understanding, elect one Speaker and one Deputy Speaker from themselves no later than twenty days after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and after the date on which the session of the Legislature-Parliament is summoned in accordance with clause (6) of Article 296 where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 3
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause (2), a member of the Legislature-Parliament who commands a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament shall be deemed to be elected as the Speaker or Deputy Speaker.
raw_text: In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause (2), a member of the Legislature-Parliament who commands a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament shall be deemed to be elected as the Speaker or Deputy Speaker.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 4
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
In holding electing in accordance with clause (2) or (3), the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker shall be members representing different political parties in the Legislature-Parliament.
raw_text: In holding electing in accordance with clause (2) or (3), the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker shall be members representing different political parties in the Legislature-Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 5
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
In discharging his or her functions in accordance with this Constitution, the Speaker or Deputy Speaker shall so discharge functions in the capacity of a neutral person without standing for or against any political party whatsoever.
raw_text: In discharging his or her functions in accordance with this Constitution, the Speaker or Deputy Speaker shall so discharge functions in the capacity of a neutral person without standing for or against any political party whatsoever.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 6
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
The office of the Speaker or Deputy Speaker shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she resigns in writing,
(b) if he or she ceases to be a member of the Legislature-Parliament,
(c) if a resolution is passed by a majority of at least two-thirds of the total number of the members of the Legislature-Parliament  to the effect that his or her conduct is not compatible with his or her office,
(d) if he or she dies.
raw_text:
The office of the Speaker or Deputy Speaker shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances:
(a) if he or she resigns in writing,
(b) if he or she ceases to be a member of the Legislature-Parliament,
(c) if a resolution is passed by a majority of at least two-thirds of the total number of the members of the Legislature-Parliament  to the effect that his or her conduct is not compatible with his or her office,
(d) if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 7
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
The Deputy Speaker or another member shall preside over a meeting at which deliberations are to be held on a resolution that the conduct of the Speaker of the Legislature-Parliament is not compatible with his or her office, and the Speaker may take part and vote in the deliberations on such resolution.
raw_text: The Deputy Speaker or another member shall preside over a meeting at which deliberations are to be held on a resolution that the conduct of the Speaker of the Legislature-Parliament is not compatible with his or her office, and the Speaker may take part and vote in the deliberations on such resolution.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text:
Part 33 Article 299
Clause 8
Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
Other procedures on the election to the Speaker and Deputy Speaker and procedures on the moving and passage of a motion that the Speaker or Deputy Speaker has committed conduct not compatible with his or her office shall be as provided by the then prevailing rules of the Legislature- Parliament.
raw_text: Other procedures on the election to the Speaker and Deputy Speaker and procedures on the moving and passage of a motion that the Speaker or Deputy Speaker has committed conduct not compatible with his or her office shall be as provided by the then prevailing rules of the Legislature- Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Judiciary
The Supreme Court, Constituent Assembly Court, Appellate Courts and District Courts existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the structure of Judiciary set forth in this Constitution is set up. Nothing in this Constitution shall be deemed to bar the settlement by respective Courts of the cases already filed in such Courts prior to the commencement of this Constitution and of the cases to be filed after the commencement of this Constitution.
raw_text: The Supreme Court, Constituent Assembly Court, Appellate Courts and District Courts existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the structure of Judiciary set forth in this Constitution is set up. Nothing in this Constitution shall be deemed to bar the settlement by respective Courts of the cases already filed in such Courts prior to the commencement of this Constitution and of the cases to be filed after the commencement of this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Judiciary
The Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court, Chief Judges and Judges of the Appellate Courts and Judges of the Districts Counts shall be deemed to have been appointed under this Constitution.
raw_text: The Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court, Chief Judges and Judges of the Appellate Courts and Judges of the Districts Counts shall be deemed to have been appointed under this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 3
Provisions relating to Judiciary
The High Courts set forth in Article 139 shall be established no later than one year after the date of commencement of this Constitution. The Appellate Courts existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be dissolved after the establishment of such Courts.
raw_text: The High Courts set forth in Article 139 shall be established no later than one year after the date of commencement of this Constitution. The Appellate Courts existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be dissolved after the establishment of such Courts.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 4
Provisions relating to Judiciary
After the establishment of the High Courts in accordance with clause (3), the cases sub judice in the Appellate Courts shall be transferred to such High Courts as specified by the Government of Nepal, in consultation with the Judicial Council, by notification in the Nepal Gazette.
raw_text: After the establishment of the High Courts in accordance with clause (3), the cases sub judice in the Appellate Courts shall be transferred to such High Courts as specified by the Government of Nepal, in consultation with the Judicial Council, by notification in the Nepal Gazette.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 5
Provisions relating to Judiciary
After the establishment of the High Courts in accordance with clause (3), the Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, post the Chief Judges and Judges of the Appellate Courts who are holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution to the offices of  the Chief Judges and Judges of the High Courts.
raw_text: After the establishment of the High Courts in accordance with clause (3), the Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, post the Chief Judges and Judges of the Appellate Courts who are holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution to the offices of  the Chief Judges and Judges of the High Courts.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 6
Provisions relating to Judiciary
The Additional Judges of the Appellate Courts who are holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution may continue to hold office until the term specified at the time of their appointment.
raw_text: The Additional Judges of the Appellate Courts who are holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution may continue to hold office until the term specified at the time of their appointment.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text:
Part 33 Article 300
Clause 7
Provisions relating to Judiciary
The cases on criminal offences punishable by imprisonment for a term of more than one year and sub judice in any body other than a Court at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall, after the commencement of this Constitution, be transferred to the concerned District Court.
raw_text: The cases on criminal offences punishable by imprisonment for a term of more than one year and sub judice in any body other than a Court at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall, after the commencement of this Constitution, be transferred to the concerned District Court.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
text:
Part 33 Article 301
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
The Constitutional Bodies which are existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution and provided in this Constitution shall be deemed to have been formed under this Constitution, and nothing shall be deemed to bar the settlement of the matters under consideration of such Bodies subject to this Constitution.
raw_text: The Constitutional Bodies which are existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution and provided in this Constitution shall be deemed to have been formed under this Constitution, and nothing shall be deemed to bar the settlement of the matters under consideration of such Bodies subject to this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
text:
Part 33 Article 301
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
The chiefs or officials of the Constitutional Bodies holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be deemed to have been appointed in accordance with this Constitution and shall continue to hold their respective offices subject to the conditions of services at the time of their appointments.
raw_text: The chiefs or officials of the Constitutional Bodies holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be deemed to have been appointed in accordance with this Constitution and shall continue to hold their respective offices subject to the conditions of services at the time of their appointments.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
text:
Part 33 Article 301
Clause 3
Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
The officials holding office in the Commission for the Investigation of the Abuse of Authority and the Public Service Commission, at the time of commencement of this Constitution, in excess of the number specified in this Constitution, shall continue to hold their respective offices subject to the conditions of services at the time of their appointments.
raw_text: The officials holding office in the Commission for the Investigation of the Abuse of Authority and the Public Service Commission, at the time of commencement of this Constitution, in excess of the number specified in this Constitution, shall continue to hold their respective offices subject to the conditions of services at the time of their appointments.

title: Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
text:
Part 33 Article 302
Clause 1
Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
The Government of Nepal shall make necessary provisions for the delivery of services at the State and Local levels.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal shall make necessary provisions for the delivery of services at the State and Local levels.

title: Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
text:
Part 33 Article 302
Clause 2
Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
The Government of Nepal may, in making provisions under clause (1), arrange for the delivery of services by making adjustment of the employees serving in the government services at the time of commencement of this Constitution with the Federal, State and Local levels in accordance with law.
raw_text: The Government of Nepal may, in making provisions under clause (1), arrange for the delivery of services by making adjustment of the employees serving in the government services at the time of commencement of this Constitution with the Federal, State and Local levels in accordance with law.

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text:
Part 33 Article 303
Clause 1
Provisions relating to Local Bodies
The Local Bodies existing at the time of Commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the determination of the number and areas of the Local level in accordance with this Constitution.
raw_text: The Local Bodies existing at the time of Commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the determination of the number and areas of the Local level in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text:
Part 33 Article 303
Clause 2
Provisions relating to Local Bodies
Election to the officials of the Local Bodies existing under clause (1) shall be held in accordance with law.
raw_text: Election to the officials of the Local Bodies existing under clause (1) shall be held in accordance with law.

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text:
Part 33 Article 303
Clause 3
Provisions relating to Local Bodies
The officials of the Local Bodies elected in accordance with clause (2) shall continue to hold office until election to the Local level is held in accordance with this Constitution.
raw_text: The officials of the Local Bodies elected in accordance with clause (2) shall continue to hold office until election to the Local level is held in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Existing laws to remain in force
text:
Part 33 Article 304
Clause 1
Existing laws to remain in force
The Nepal laws in force at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to be in force until such laws are repealed or amended.
Provided that any law which is inconsistent with this Constitution shall ipso facto be invalid to the extent of such inconsistency, after one year of the date on which the first session of the Federal Parliament set forth in this Constitution is held.
raw_text:
The Nepal laws in force at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to be in force until such laws are repealed or amended.
Provided that any law which is inconsistent with this Constitution shall ipso facto be invalid to the extent of such inconsistency, after one year of the date on which the first session of the Federal Parliament set forth in this Constitution is held.

title: Existing laws to remain in force
text:
Part 33 Article 304
Clause 2
Existing laws to remain in force
Acts relating to peace process under the Interim Constitution of Nepal, 2007 (2063) shall be deemed to have been done under this Constitution.
raw_text: Acts relating to peace process under the Interim Constitution of Nepal, 2007 (2063) shall be deemed to have been done under this Constitution.

title: Power to remove difficulties
text:
Part 33 Article 305
Clause 1
Power to remove difficulties
If any difficulty arises in connection with the implementation of this Constitution until the commencement of the first session of the Federal Parliament, upon being elected in accordance with this Constitution, the President may, on the recommendation of the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers, issue necessary orders to remove such difficulty; and such orders must be submitted to the Legislature-Parliament or Federal Parliament, held immediately after the issue such orders, for approval
raw_text: If any difficulty arises in connection with the implementation of this Constitution until the commencement of the first session of the Federal Parliament, upon being elected in accordance with this Constitution, the President may, on the recommendation of the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers, issue necessary orders to remove such difficulty; and such orders must be submitted to the Legislature-Parliament or Federal Parliament, held immediately after the issue such orders, for approval

title: Definitions and interpretations
text:
Part 34 Article 306
Clause 1
Definitions and interpretations
Unless the subject or the context otherwise requires, in this Constitution,-
(a) "minorities" means ethnic, linguistic and religious groups whose population is less than the percentage specified by the Federal law, and includes groups that have their distinct ethnic, religious or linguistic characteristics, aspirations to protect such features and subjected to discrimination and oppression,
(b) "law" means a Federal law, State law and Local law,
(c) "Article" means an article of this Constitution,
(d) "Municipality" means and includes a Municipal Corporation and Sub-municipal Corporation.
(e) "citizen" means a citizen of Nepal,
(f) "State" means the area and form of a federal unit of Nepal divided into the Federal units in accordance with this Constitution,
(g) "remuneration" means and includes salary, allowance, pension and any other form of emolument and facility,
(h) "state power" means the power relating to the Executive, Legislative and Judiciary of the state, and includes residual power.
(i) "Bill" means a draft of amendment to the Constitution or of an Act which has been introduced in the Federal Parliament or a State Assembly,
(j) "Federation" means the federal level that is the apex unit of the federal structure,
(k) "Federal Units" means the Federation, State and Local levels,
(l) "Constitutional Bodies" means the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority, Auditor General, Public Service Commission, Election Commission, National Human Rights Commission, National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission, National Women Commission, National Dalit Commission, National Inclusion Commission, Indigenous Nationalities Commission, Madhesi Commission, Tharu Commission and Muslim Commission,
(m) "marginalized" means communities that are made politically, economically and socially backward, are unable to enjoy services and facilities because of discrimination and oppression and of geographical remoteness or deprived thereof and are in lower status than the human development standards mentioned in Federal law, and includes highly marginalized groups and groups on the verge of extinction,
(n) "Local level" means the Village Bodies, Municipalities and District Assemblies to be established in accordance with this Constitution.
raw_text:
Unless the subject or the context otherwise requires, in this Constitution,-
(a) "minorities" means ethnic, linguistic and religious groups whose population is less than the percentage specified by the Federal law, and includes groups that have their distinct ethnic, religious or linguistic characteristics, aspirations to protect such features and subjected to discrimination and oppression,
(b) "law" means a Federal law, State law and Local law,
(c) "Article" means an article of this Constitution,
(d) "Municipality" means and includes a Municipal Corporation and Sub-municipal Corporation.
(e) "citizen" means a citizen of Nepal,
(f) "State" means the area and form of a federal unit of Nepal divided into the Federal units in accordance with this Constitution,
(g) "remuneration" means and includes salary, allowance, pension and any other form of emolument and facility,
(h) "state power" means the power relating to the Executive, Legislative and Judiciary of the state, and includes residual power.
(i) "Bill" means a draft of amendment to the Constitution or of an Act which has been introduced in the Federal Parliament or a State Assembly,
(j) "Federation" means the federal level that is the apex unit of the federal structure,
(k) "Federal Units" means the Federation, State and Local levels,
(l) "Constitutional Bodies" means the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority, Auditor General, Public Service Commission, Election Commission, National Human Rights Commission, National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission, National Women Commission, National Dalit Commission, National Inclusion Commission, Indigenous Nationalities Commission, Madhesi Commission, Tharu Commission and Muslim Commission,
(m) "marginalized" means communities that are made politically, economically and socially backward, are unable to enjoy services and facilities because of discrimination and oppression and of geographical remoteness or deprived thereof and are in lower status than the human development standards mentioned in Federal law, and includes highly marginalized groups and groups on the verge of extinction,
(n) "Local level" means the Village Bodies, Municipalities and District Assemblies to be established in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Definitions and interpretations
text:
Part 34 Article 306
Clause 2
Definitions and interpretations
Unless the subject or the context otherwise requires, the legal provisions relating to interpretation of laws shall, subject to the provisions of this Constitution, apply to the interpretation of this Constitution in the same manner as that provisions apply to the interpretation of the Nepal laws
raw_text: Unless the subject or the context otherwise requires, the legal provisions relating to interpretation of laws shall, subject to the provisions of this Constitution, apply to the interpretation of this Constitution in the same manner as that provisions apply to the interpretation of the Nepal laws

title: Short title and commencement
text:
Part 35 Article 307
Clause 1
Short title and commencement
This Constitution may be cited as the "Constitution of Nepal".
raw_text: This Constitution may be cited as the "Constitution of Nepal".

title: Short title and commencement
text:
Part 35 Article 307
Clause 2
Short title and commencement
This Constitution shall commence on 20 September 2015 (3rd day of the month of Ashwin of the year 2072 Bikram Sambat).
raw_text: This Constitution shall commence on 20 September 2015 (3rd day of the month of Ashwin of the year 2072 Bikram Sambat).

title: Repeal
text:
Part 35 Article 308
Repeal
The Interim Constitution of Nepal, 2007 (2063) is hereby repealed.
raw_text: The Interim Constitution of Nepal, 2007 (2063) is hereby repealed.
